program_outcomes: Temel bilimler, temel mühendislik ve bilgisayar mühendisliği tasarım ilke ve yöntemleri ile ilgili yeterli bilgi birikimi, ve bunları karmaşık mühendislik problemlerinin modellenmesi ve çözümü için uygulayabilme becerisi,
Ayrık matematik kavram ve konularını uygulayabilme becerisi,
Karmaşık mühendislik problemlerini tanımlama, veri toplama, yorumlama, problemleri analiz etme, modelleme ve etkin çözümler geliştirme ve uygulama becerisi,
Donanım ve yazılım bileşenleri ile bir bilgisayar sisteminin, gerçekçi kısıtlar ve koşullar altında, analizini, tasarımını ve yönetimini modern mühendislik yöntemleri ile gerçekleştirebilme becerisi,

Mühendislik uygulamalarında karşılaşılan karmaşık problemlerin analizi ve çözümü için, modern mühendislik teknik ve araçlarını, bilişim teknolojileri ve yazılımlarını geliştirme, seçme ve etkin bir şekilde kullanabilme becerisi,

Karmaşık mühendislik problemlerinin veya bilgisayar mühendisliği alanındaki araştırma konularının incelenmesi için laboratuvar ve bilgisayar ortamında deney tasarlama, deney yapma, veri toplama, sonuçları analiz etme ve yorumlama becerisi,
Tek ve çok disiplinli takım çalışması yürütebilme becerisi, buna yönelik bireysel becerilere de sahip olma,

Türkçe ve İngilizce olarak, yazılı ve sözlü etkili iletişim kurabilme becerisi, etkin rapor yazma ve yazılı raporları anlama, tasarım ve üretim raporları hazırlayabilme, etkin sunum yapabilme, açık ve anlaşılır talimat verme ve alma becerisi,
Kendi alanındaki uluslararası çalışmaları takip edebilme becerisi,

Yaşam boyu öğrenmenin gerekliliği bilinci, bilimi ve teknolojideki gelişmeleri izleyerek kendini sürekli yenileyebilme becerisi, etik ilkelerine uygun davranma, mesleki ve etik sorumluluk bilinci, mühendislik uygulamalarında kullanılan standartlar hakkında bilgi,
Proje yönetimi, risk yönetimi ve değişiklik yönetimi gibi, iş hayatındaki uygulamalar ve sürdürülebilir kalkınma hakkında bilgi; girişimcilik, yenilikçilik hakkında farkındalık,

Evrensel ve toplumsal sorunlara duyarlılık, çağın mühendislik alanına yansıyan sorunları hakkında ve mühendislik çözümlerinin sağlık, çevre, güvenlikle ilgili etkileri konusunda bilgi ve hukuksal sonuçları konusunda farkındalık.

courses.name: Technical Writing and Presentation
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide general and task-specific knowledge related to written and oral presentations. It teaches general and engineering-specific techniques for preparing reports, thesis texts, project proposals, posters, and academic emails in written communication, and for preparing slides and delivering oral presentations in oral communication. Negotiation and interview techniques are also discussed.
courses.description: The course covers general and technical writing techniques, oral presentation, negotiation, plagiarism, engineering ethics, and related topics.
learning_outcomes: Learn how to write academic reports, project proposals, and emails.
Learn how to prepare slides and deliver a good oral presentation in academic settings.
Learn about ethics and plagiarism in the academic world.
Learn how to prepare for an interview.
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: What is good writing and a good writer?
Hafta 6: How are punctuation marks used?
Hafta 7: How to prepare a report
Hafta 8: How to write sentences and paragraphs?
Hafta 9: How to search in search engines?
resources: Alan S. Pringle and Sarah S, Technical Writing 101: A Real-World Guide to Planning and Writing Technical Content, 2009
A.M. Einspruch, Tech Writing Tips: A Practical Guide for Technical People, 2023
Ders slaytları
https://online.stanford.edu/courses/som-y0010-writing-sciences

courses.name: Engineering Mathematics I
courses.purpose: This course provides a comprehensive introduction to engineering mathematics with an emphasis on engineering applications. Students will work with core concepts such as limits, continuity, derivatives, and integrals while developing their mathematical skills and their ability to apply them to real-world engineering problems.
courses.description: Review of functions (definition and intervals, graphing and interpretation, special functions such as exponential and trigonometric functions), limits, continuity, applications of limits in engineering problems, derivatives, applications of derivatives in engineering problems, integrals, integration techniques, overview of numerical integration, definite integrals, improper integrals, and applications of integrals in engineering problems.
learning_outcomes: Analyze and solve engineering problems using limits, derivatives, and integrals.
Develop critical thinking and problem-solving skills required for engineering applications.
Use computer tools in solving engineering problems.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Functions (polynomials, trigonometric functions, exponential functions, logarithm, graphing with Desmos)
Hafta 2: Functions (polynomials, trigonometric functions, exponential functions, logarithm, graphing with Desmos)
Hafta 6: Applications of Derivatives (e.g., optimization problems in engineering)
Hafta 7: Applications of Derivatives (e.g., optimization problems in engineering)
Hafta 8: Integrals (indefinite integrals of polynomials and trigonometric, exp/log functions; using online integration tools such as Wolfram)
Hafta 9: Definite Integrals (use online integration tools such as Wolfram)
Hafta 13: Applications of Definite Integrals (e.g., solving engineering problems - volume or area calculations)
Hafta 14: Applications of Definite Integrals (e.g., solving engineering problems - volume or area calculations)
resources: Thomas Calculus (13th edition); G. B. Thomas, M. D. Weir, J. R. Hass

courses.name: Linear Algebra
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with the fundamentals of matrix theory, which plays an important role in basic sciences and engineering, and its physics-engineering applications. It also aims to inform students about the solutions of linear systems of equations, help them understand the algebraic structure of matrices, and use this understanding to determine the properties of vector spaces.
courses.description: Linear systems of equations. Matrices, matrix operations, special types of matrices. Echelon form of a matrix, Gauss-Jordan elimination. Elementary matrices and the inverse of a matrix. Determinants, Cramer's rule. Vector spaces and subspaces. Linear combinations, linear independence. Basis and dimension, rank of a matrix. Eigenvalues and corresponding eigenvectors. Diagonalization. Orthogonality. Gram-Schmidt orthogonalization method. Physics and engineering applications.
learning_outcomes: Solve linear systems of equations
Perform matrix operations and find the inverse of a matrix
Solve matrix equations
Compute determinants
Use Cramer's rule
Understand vector spaces and subspaces algebraically and geometrically
Find the basis and dimension of a vector space
Identify eigenvalues and corresponding eigenvectors
Perform diagonalization
Apply the Gram-Schmidt orthogonalization method
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to the course, linear and nonlinear systems
resources: D.C. Lay (2000). Linear Algebra and Its Applications, 2nd ed., Addison Wesley
D. Zelinsky, A First Course in Linear Algebra, Academic Press.

courses.name: Physics I
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to the fundamental concepts of classical mechanics and present theory and applications in a clear and understandable way. Students are motivated through solved problems and homework assignments.
courses.description: Measurement, vectors, motion in one dimension, motion in two dimensions, Newton's laws of motion, applications of Newton's laws, Newton's law of universal gravitation, work and energy, conservation of energy, momentum and motion of systems, static equilibrium of rigid bodies, rotation I and rotation II.
learning_outcomes: To develop the ability to perform dimensional analysis and unit conversions.
To learn scalar and vector products.
To apply Newton's laws of motion to mechanics problems.
To solve basic problems using the work-energy theorem.
To solve mechanics problems according to the principle of conservation of energy.
To learn the concept of momentum and perform applications of the impulse-momentum theorem.
To understand the mechanics of rigid bodies rotating about a fixed axis and learn rotational motion and the concept of torque.

In the laboratory environment: dimensional analysis and unit conversion; scalar and vector products; application of Newton's laws of motion to mechanics problems; solving basic problems using the work-energy theorem; solving mechanics problems according to the principle of conservation of energy; mechanics of rigid bodies rotating about a fixed axis; rotational motion.
weekly_topics: Hafta 4: Motion in two and three dimensions
Hafta 11: Particle systems and center of mass
resources: Physics I (Mechanics), F.J. Keller, W. E. Gettys, M. J. Skove, McGraw-Hill, 2006
PRINCIPLES of PHYSICS, 9th Ed., Halliday, Resnick, J. Walker, J. Wiley Pub., 2011
Sears and Zemansky's UNIVERSITY PHYSICS, 13th Ed., HD Young, RA Freedman, Pearson Pub., 2011
PHYSICS for Scientists and Engineers, 3th Ed., PM Fisbane, SG Gasiorowicz, ST Thornton, Pearson Pub., 2005

courses.name: Humanity's History
courses.purpose: The main purpose of this course is to introduce students to fundamental concepts related to world civilizations and history in the humanities and social sciences. It also aims to help students develop critical thinking and analytical capacities, learn how to read primary texts, express arguments and ideas orally, visually, and in writing, and cultivate intellectual responsibility and respect toward others.
courses.description: KHAS103 aims to raise students' awareness of the dominant ways of relating to the past in history and the social sciences, and to help them understand the problematic and contested nature of our relationship with the past. The course covers the history of modern societies from the eighteenth century to the present and is designed around overlapping objectives. It focuses on fissures in historiography, introduces themes that inform broader debates about how history should be understood, and centers on the concept of historical agency. Each topic asks what room there is for agency, what limits are placed on it, and what the main forms of historical agency are and how they compete with one another. The course also includes weekly content introductions and weekly critical skills training to support students' active participation in these debates.
learning_outcomes: Gain a deeper understanding of the broader human community we live in, including its shared history, inherited values, cultural and ethnic diversity, and the ideological conflicts and ethical dilemmas that continue to shape our approach to it.

Understand our relationship with the past as a polyphonic structure by synthesizing competing perspectives and methods from different sources and reaching independent conclusions on the key questions of each topic.

Develop academic writing skills by writing a paper structured according to academic conventions and demonstrating speaking skills that provide a critical and creative response to contemporary academic debates.
Increase awareness of agency in academic and non-academic contexts by understanding the extent to which freedom and responsibility can be exercised in our world.
Take individual responsibility and work collaboratively with other students in completing group assignments.
resources: Short readings supporting the course will be assigned each week.
Primary sources and films supporting discussion sessions will be recommended.

courses.name: Evrensel Değerler ve Etik
courses.purpose: KHAS 105, students to think critically and without prejudice about current issues and ethical dilemmas. Throughout the course, students use informal logic to understand, analyze, and discuss topics related to gender and diversity, environmental ethics, bioethics, and information technology. Lectures and discussions with professors from various academic disciplines, together with weekly sessions led by instructors who teach academic skills development, provide an introduction and resources for topics and discussions that are the subject of critical analysis.
courses.description: The course consists of an introduction in the first week of the semester, followed by 4 topic modules, each lasting approximately 2-3 weeks. In one week, the course provides the necessary content information and resources for discussions and arguments related to the module. In the following week, it aims to help students develop the skills needed to think critically and creatively about the knowledge and arguments they acquired in the previous week and to reinforce the topics.
learning_outcomes: Demonstrate a high awareness of universal values and evaluate the personal and social responsibilities of living in a world where diversity exists.
Understand the importance of adopting a fact, perspective, or thought independently of others' viewpoints and judgments.
Effectively use mental and discursive tools to respond creatively and critically to current issues and ethical dilemmas.
Distinguish and evaluate scientifically qualified texts and popular texts.
Critically question personal beliefs and biases.
Demonstrate proficiency in identifying nuances of word choice and manipulative language.
weekly_topics: Hafta 12: Skills: Ethics of Information Technologies
resources: Selected short readings appropriate to the main themes of the course
Short films, newspaper articles, radio broadcasts

courses.name: English Critical Reading and Writing I
courses.purpose: This course aims to develop students' ability to express themselves effectively in spoken and written Turkish in everyday life, to read argumentative texts of different lengths with understanding, and to produce critical written and oral comments on what they read. It also encourages students to express their ideas and arguments individually or within a group in accordance with the rules of discussion, while developing the habit of accessing accurate printed, digital, and visual sources for academic research and applying the principles of academic integrity in their written and oral production.
courses.description: This course is designed as a 2-hour weekly reading and writing workshop to improve students' Turkish reading and writing skills and enable them to analyze what they read, hear, and watch from a critical perspective and express their thoughts in clear and fluent Turkish. The course is intended to develop students' ability to express themselves effectively in spoken and written form, read and understand argumentative texts ranging from short paragraphs to long articles, produce critical comments, construct arguments on a given topic, conduct academic research, access accurate printed and digital sources, and express their interpretations in written and oral form while observing academic ethics.
learning_outcomes: They can express themselves clearly and effectively in everyday life using proper Turkish.
They can read argumentative texts of different lengths with understanding and produce their own critical comments.
They can negotiate their ideas individually or as a member of a group in accordance with discussion rules.
They can access accurate sources for academic research.
They apply the principles of academic integrity in their written and oral production.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Course and syllabus introduction, getting acquainted
Hafta 4: Proposition / paragraph in academic writing
Hafta 6: Discussion on a documentary and paragraph writing
Hafta 8: Research week - determining debate groups
Hafta 11: In-class debate report preparation workshop
resources: Afganistanbul, Director Ulaş Tosun, 2018 (https://vimeo.com/348987107/86017095b)
Filmmor – What is Honor? (15')
Enis Batur, "Okuyazmak", Yazboz, Sel Yayıncılık, 2001.
Khas Learn course notes
Normand Baillargeon, Eleştirel Düşünme Kılavuzu, Dipnot, 2007.

courses.name: Computer Programming I
courses.purpose: This course will help students understand the role computation can play in solving problems. It will help students with little or no programming experience gain confidence in writing small programs that achieve useful goals. The course also provides context for common features of programming languages such as variables, control structures, and functions by teaching students the working principles of a computer.
courses.description: The course generally introduces students to the world of programming and helps them design algorithms for problems and convert these algorithms into code in the C programming language.
learning_outcomes: Conceptualize what happens inside a computer during execution
Work easily with binary, octal, and hexadecimal representations
Have an understanding of how data is represented in machines
Read and write algorithms, pseudocode, and flowcharts
Write small programs in C
Track a program's state during execution and use debugging
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to algorithms and flowcharts
Hafta 2: Practice on algorithms and flowcharts
Hafta 3: Structure of a program in C
resources: C, How to Program, By Paul J. Deitel, Harvey M. Deitel · 2010
Computer Fundamentals and Programming in C, Pradip Dey, Manas Ghosh, 2. edition (2013), Oxford University Press
The C Programming Language, Brian Kernighan and Dennis Ritchie, 2. edition (1978), Prentice Hall Software Series

courses.name: Engineering Mathematics II
courses.purpose: To provide the fundamental mathematical tools required for engineering applications.
courses.description: Applications of indefinite and definite integrals, integration techniques, rational functions, generalized integrals, numerical integration, vectors and their applications, equations of lines and planes in space, and an introduction to multivariable functions including limits, continuity, partial derivatives, and differential forms.
learning_outcomes: Compute indefinite integrals of elementary functions.
Use the Mean Value Theorem for Integrals and the Fundamental Theorem of Calculus and apply them.
Calculate plane areas, arc lengths, volumes of solids of revolution, and surface areas.
Analyze the convergence and divergence of generalized integrals.
Use numerical integration methods such as the Trapezoidal Rule and Simpson's Rule.
Perform basic operations on vectors and use them to write equations of lines and planes in space.
Examine the limits and continuity of multivariable functions and compute partial derivatives.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Applications of definite integrals, volumes; Sections 6.1-6.2; group work in class on volume calculations
Hafta 2: Applications of definite integrals, arc lengths and areas; Sections 6.3-6.4; group work in class on arc length and surface area calculations
Hafta 3: Logarithmic and exponential functions; exponential change; Sections 7.2, 7.3, 7.4; group work in class on exponential change
Hafta 4: Applications of integrals; use of open-access web resources and AI tools for advanced applications of integrals; Sections 8.1-8.4; group work in class on the use of AI tools for integration
Hafta 5: Applications of integrals; use of open-access web resources and AI tools for advanced applications of integrals; Sections 8.5-8.6; group work in class on the use of AI tools for integration
Hafta 6: Numerical integration using Simpson's rule; Section 8.7; group work in class on numerical integration
Hafta 8: Complex numbers; Appendix A.7; group work in class on the use of complex numbers in engineering
Hafta 9: Polar coordinates and applications; Sections 11.3-11.4; group work in class on the use of polar coordinates in engineering
Hafta 10: Vectors and geometry of 3-space; Sections 12.1-12.4; group work in class on the use of vectors in engineering
Hafta 11: Equations of lines, planes, cylinders, and quadric surfaces in space; Sections 12.5-12.6; group work in class on the use of vectors in engineering
Hafta 12: Infinite sequences and series; Sections 10.1-10.2; group work in class on the application of infinite series in engineering
Hafta 13: Power series, Taylor-Maclaurin series; Sections 10.7-10.8; group work in class on the application of power series in engineering
Hafta 14: Approximation of functions by Taylor series; Section 10.9; group work in class on linear and quadratic approximations
resources: Thomas’ Calculus, George B. Thomas, Jr., Maurice D. Weir, Christopher Heil, Antonio Behn, Pearson, Ed. 13

courses.name: Complexity in Natural Sciences
courses.purpose: Students will develop an understanding of complex systems in the natural sciences by exploring fundamental concepts such as emergence, self-organization, and nonlinear interactions. Through interdisciplinary approaches, they will learn to analyze and model complex phenomena and strengthen their critical thinking skills about dynamic and interconnected systems.
courses.description: This course introduces the thinking and modeling of Complex Systems, covering core concepts such as dynamical systems and chaos, fractals, complex networks, probability and information theory, computational complexity, and cellular automata. Designed as an introductory exploration, the course emphasizes understanding the big picture of complex systems and provides students with foundational insights into their dynamics and connections.
learning_outcomes: Understand the fundamental concepts of Complex Systems
Ability to apply complex systems thinking in natural and basic sciences
Ability to use modeling concepts, tools, and techniques
Ability to think critically in evaluating real-world systems
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to Complex Systems: Why and What? A broad introduction to complex systems, their importance, why they should be studied, and their impact on the evolution of scientific research methodologies.

Hafta 2: A Modeling Perspective: The Rules and Roles of Natural Laws An applied exploration of agent-based and equation-based modeling of a pandemic model (SIR model) using NetLogo; the role of simple rules and equations in complex phenomena.

Hafta 3: Formalizing Natural Laws and the Limits of Mathematical Systems An introduction to formal thinking through Turing machines and an examination of their capacity for intelligent behavior and the fundamental limits of computation.
Hafta 4: On the Importance of Connections: Introduction to Networks An introduction to the basic properties of networks and graphs and how they emerge as a natural component of complex systems.

Hafta 5: The Doctrine of Chance: Real-World Networks and Random Graphs An introduction to probability theory and probability distributions, as well as a general overview of information theory, random graphs, and their applications in studying real-world networks.

Hafta 6: Gresham, Pareto, and Beckstrom: Complexity in Economic Networks A complexity-theory perspective on economics as one of the most complex networks, including analysis of system dynamics such as Epstein’s Sugarscape model and Schelling’s segregation model.

Hafta 7: Complexity Perspective in Finance and Management: Newton and the Madness of Crowds A brief introduction to network effects and the importance of Metcalfe’s and Reed’s laws in understanding the spread of innovation across networks and how they can be used for growth.

Hafta 8: Critical Points, Phase Transitions, and Bifurcation: Introduction to Dynamical Systems A mathematical examination of how systems evolve over time, how they are affected by internal and external factors, and sudden quantitative changes at critical points.

Hafta 9: From Fibonacci’s Rabbits to the Lyapunov Brothers: Logistic Map An examination of a population growth model, the emergence of bifurcation diagrams, and their significance; also criteria for detecting chaos in natural systems.

Hafta 10: Self-Similarity, Cantor Set, Fractals: A Look at the Paradoxes of Infinity An exploration of self-similar structures, their relationship to chaos and complexity, and how they challenge our intuitive understanding of natural phenomena.

Hafta 11: Emergence, Self-Organized Criticality, and Cellular Automata Complexity as the science of emergence, along with cellular automata and visualizations. In this context, the true meaning of sensitivity to initial conditions will also be explored.

Hafta 12: Conway’s Game of Life: The Glorification of Complexity An examination of Conway’s Game of Life, a two-dimensional cellular automaton, and its connection to complexity theory; emphasizing how complex systems can reproduce themselves.

Hafta 13: Homo Ludens: Game Theory, Evolution, and Genetic Algorithms An analysis of interactions among players in a system from the perspective of game theory; how players interact with each other, learn from experience, and how these interactions lead to complex systems.
Hafta 14: Final Evaluations and Course Review A review of the topics covered and a broader perspective on complexity theory.
resources: Mitchell, M. (2009). Complexity: A Guided Tour. Oxford University Press.
Feldman, D. P. (2012). Chaos and Fractals: An Elementary Introduction. Oxford University Press.
Pósfai, M., & Barabási, A. L. (2016). Network Science. Cambridge, UK, Cambridge University Press.
Bak, P. (2013). How Nature Works: The Science of Self-organized Criticality. Springer Science & Business Media.
Hawkins, J. (2024). The Mathematics of Cellular Automata (Vol. 108). American Mathematical Society.
Holland, J. H. (2014). Complexity: A Very Short Introduction. Oxford University Press.
Meadows, D. H. (2008). Thinking in Systems: A Primer. Sustainability Institute.
Sipser, M. (1996). Introduction to the Theory of Computation. ACM Sigact News, 27(1), 27-29.

courses.name: Physics II
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to the fundamental concepts of classical mechanics and present theory and applications in a clear and understandable way. Students are motivated through solved problems and assignments.
courses.description: Coulomb's law and electric field, Gauss's law, electric potential, capacitance, electric energy and properties of insulators, current and resistance, energy and current in DC circuits, magnetic field and sources of magnetic field, Faraday's law, inductance, magnetic field inside materials, electromagnetic oscillations and AC circuits.
learning_outcomes: Understand Coulomb's law and the concept of electric field.
Solve problems using Gauss's law.
Understand the concept of electric potential.
Learn capacitance, electric energy, and insulators.
Clarify the flow of charge and resistance using Ohm's law.
Understand energy and current in DC circuits.
Introduce the concept of magnetic field.
Develop the idea of sources of magnetic field.
Solve problems using Faraday's law.
Learn the concept of inductance.

In the laboratory environment: solve problems using Coulomb's law; understand the electric field concept; understand electric potential; learn capacitance, electric energy, and insulators; clarify the flow of charge and resistance using Ohm's law; calculate current using Kirchhoff's rule; solve problems using Biot-Savart law; solve problems using Faraday's law; learn the concept of induction.
resources: Physics I (Mechanics), F.J. Keller, W. E. Gettys, M. J. Skove, McGraw-Hill, 2006
PRINCIPLES of PHYSICS, 9th Ed., Halliday, Resnick, J. Walker, J. Wiley Pub., 2011
Sears and Zemansky?s UNIVERSITY PHYSICS, 13th Ed., HD Young, RA Freedman, Pearson Pub., 2011
PHYSICS for Scientists and Engineers, 3th Ed., PM Fisbane, SG Gasiorowicz, ST Thornton, Pearson Pub., 2005

courses.name: Toplumsal Sorumluluk Projesi
courses.purpose: Bu ders, öğrencilere toplumsal sorumluluk kavramını teorik bilgiler ve evrensel değerlerle tanıtır. Sivil katılım faaliyetleri aracılığıyla bu bilgileri aktif vatandaşlık becerilerine dönüştürmeyi amaçlar.
courses.description: Bu ders öğrencilere sivil katılım ve aktif vatandaşlık kavramlarını toplumsal sorumluluk çerçevesinde tanıtır. Öğrencilere toplumsal sorumluluk alanındaki temel bilgi ve anlayış teorik olarak sunulur. Öğrenciler teorik bilgilere paralel olarak yaşadıkları toplumun sorunlarını farkına varma, bu sorunlara çözüm üretme becerilerini geliştirmek üzere bir proje tasarlayıp yürütürler.
learning_outcomes: Sivil katılım ve aktif vatandaşlık kavramlarını tanımlayabilirler.
Evrensel değerlere uygun toplumsal sorumluluk farkındalığı kazanırlar.
Yaşadıkları çevrenin toplumsal ihtiyaçlarına uygun sorumluluk projesi tasarlar, uygular ve paylaşırlar.
Ders kapsamındaki konular hakkında fikir üreterek kendilerini yazılı ve sözlü olarak ifade edebilirler.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Giriş, Sivil Toplum Kuruluşu (STK) ve proje havuzunun tanıtılması
Hafta 2: Grup bilgilendirme - Proje önerisi ve planı / STK ile ilgili sunum hakkında bilgilendirme
Hafta 3: Proje önerisi ve planı / STK ile ilgili sunumlar
Hafta 5: Haftalık Faaliyet Raporu Sunumları – Kavram sunumları
Hafta 7: Haftalık Faaliyet Raporu Sunumları – Kavram sunumları
Hafta 10: Haftalık Faaliyet Raporu Sunumları – Kavram sunumları
Hafta 12: Haftalık Faaliyet Raporu Sunumları – Kavram sunumları
Hafta 14: Haftalık Faaliyet Raporu Sunumları – Kavram sunumları

courses.name: Turkish Critical Reading and Writing II
courses.purpose: The main purpose of this course is to identify the elements of fiction such as character, plot, point of view, description, time, and space, and to read and interpret Turkish novels and short stories analytically. It also aims to help students write critical articles using secondary sources and to develop storytelling, description, and plotting skills through short creative writing exercises. In addition, the course examines how novels and short stories are expressed through different media and their relationships with other genres such as cinema, theatre, TV series, manga, and Storytel.
courses.description: The course content is mainly based on the novel and short story genres. Through examples from modern Turkish literature, it focuses on the concept of fiction and aims for students to examine, discuss, and interpret the features, elements, and rules of fiction, including its occasional rule-breaking, from a critical perspective. The relationship of the novel and short story with other texts and genres is also discussed through examples, and students' creative writing skills are encouraged through in-class and out-of-class activities.
learning_outcomes: They can define the elements of fiction.
They can read Turkish novels and short stories critically and write critical articles.
They can produce interpretations about the relationship between the novel and short story and other genres.
They develop their skills in fiction through creative writing exercises.
They can express their interpretations in written and oral form in proper Turkish.
They apply the principles of academic integrity in their written and oral productions.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: What is fiction? Boundaries, rules, violations
Hafta 2: Introduction to the elements of fiction
resources: Murat Gülsoy, Büyübozumu: Yaratıcı Yazarlık (İstanbul: Can Yayınları, 2016)
Adalet Ağaoğlu, Ölmeye Yatmak (İstanbul: Everest, 2016)
Fatih Altuğ, Kapalı İktisat Açık Metin (İstanbul: Everest, 2018)
Sait Faik, Havada Bulut (İstanbul: Türkiye İş Bankası Kültür Yayınları, 2019)
Wayne C. Booth, Kurmacanın Retoriği (İstanbul: Metis Yayınları, 2017)

courses.name: Discrete Computational Structures
courses.purpose: The main purpose of this course is to provide students with the theoretical foundations of problem solving and to develop the ability to use discrete computational structures that are widely used in computer science and computational problems.
courses.description: Introduction to the basic concepts of discrete computational structures. Overview of formal tools needed for mathematical reasoning and proof construction. Detailed study of mathematical induction as a proof technique, a problem-solving method, an algorithm design method, and a program verification technique. Introduction to combinatorial analysis and its application to discrete structures such as sets, permutations, graphs, and trees.
learning_outcomes: Understand the basic principles of formal mathematical reasoning.
Prove theorems related to discrete structures.
Understand mathematical induction as one of the fundamental proof techniques in discrete structures.
Develop solutions and algorithms for computational problems using mathematical induction.
Prove code correctness using the proof techniques learned.

Acquire basic knowledge of fundamental structures such as sets, permutations, graphs, and trees in computer science and engineering, and use the learned proof/analysis techniques in problems related to these structures.
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: Operations and equivalences in logical propositions
Hafta 3: Predicate logic and introduction to quantifiers
Hafta 4: Logical inference rules and introduction to proofs
Hafta 6: Proving theorems in combinatorial structures such as sets and sequences using proof techniques and strategies
Hafta 7: Introduction to mathematical induction. Proving by induction
Hafta 8: Proving by induction. Using induction to prove properties of structures such as sets and sequences
Hafta 9: Relationship between induction and recursive algorithms. Developing algorithms and program verification using induction
Hafta 10: Fundamentals of counting. The pigeonhole principle
Hafta 12: Recurrence relations and solving recurrence relations
Hafta 13: Solving problems by establishing recurrence relations and its relation to induction
Hafta 14: Introduction to trees and proof of some theorems related to trees
resources: Discrete Mathematics and Its Applications, Kenneth H. Rosen, McGraw Hill 6th edition, 2007
Discrete mathematics with graph theory, Edgar G. Goodaire, Michael M. Parmenter. Pearson Prentice Hall, 2006.
Discrete and Combinatorial Mathematics, Ralph P. Grimaldi, Addison Wesley, 2004.

courses.name: Computer Programming II
courses.purpose: This course forms the foundation for programmers and software engineers. It introduces students to fundamental computer programming and coding concepts and develops problem-solving skills. Students learn core computer science topics such as computer architecture, number systems, and memory management, and gain an introduction to C++, pointers, dynamic memory allocation, file handling, and object-oriented programming.
courses.description: Introduction to computer programming, structure of a program, compilers, number systems, Boolean operators, basics of C++, expressions and flow control, computer architecture, functions, scope, arrays, strings, pointers, dynamic memory management, file input/output, preprocessor, header files, overloading, templates, exception handling, data structures, and object-oriented programming.
learning_outcomes: Ability to improve problem-solving skills
Ability to know how data is represented in memory and how programs work in memory
Ability to learn the basics of coding and be fully prepared for advanced topics
Ability to dynamically allocate memory and use pointers to read/write files
Ability to learn object-oriented programming
Ability to develop software and projects in C++
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction, Hello World, Storing Data in Memory, Basic C++
Hafta 2: Functions, Functions in Memory, Templates
Hafta 3: Scope, Arrays, Matrix, Exceptions, Headers
resources: Herbert Schildt. C++: The complete reference. McGraw-Hill Education; 4th edition (December 10, 2002).
Paul J. Deitel, and Harvey M. Deitel. C++: how to program. Pearson; 10th edition (February 29, 2016).
Bjarne Stroustrup. The C++ Programming Language. Addison-Wesley Professional; 4th edition (May 9, 2013).
C++ full referans: https://www.cplusplus.com
Online derleyici: https://app.coderpad.io/sandbox

courses.name: Digital Circuit Design
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce Boolean algebra and the fundamental analysis and synthesis methods for logic circuits in a project-based context. Both combinational and sequential circuits are covered through various design examples.
courses.description: Number systems; Boolean algebra; logic networks and simplification; logic design techniques using gates and MSI chips; combinational circuits; basic sequential circuits; D/A and A/D conversion; design examples.
learning_outcomes: Understand the operating principles of commonly used digital electronic circuit elements.
Apply basic analysis methods for digital electronic circuits.
Analyze, design, and implement simple electronic circuits using these elements, and use the basic measuring instruments and tools required for this purpose.
Use Boolean algebra and logic functions, simplify them, and derive circuit diagrams.
Analyze and synthesize basic combinational and sequential logic functions at the circuit diagram level.
Design, project, and implement circuits related to complex Boolean functions using basic logic structures and other MSI chips.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction, number systems, complement arithmetic, codes
Hafta 2: Axiomatic definition of Boolean algebra, basic theorems, canonical and standard forms, basic logic gates AND, OR, NOT, EXOR, EXNOR
Hafta 3: Project I (Basic application with Combinational Logic Circuit and implementation)
Hafta 4: Simplification of logic functions, implementation with NAND and NOR gates
Hafta 5: Fault detection and correction, design examples with MSI chips
Hafta 6: Adders, subtractor circuits, comparators, and code converters
Hafta 7: Project II (Sequential Logic Project), Project I presentations
Hafta 8: Implementation of logic functions with multiplexers
Hafta 9: Implementation of logic functions with ROM circuits
Hafta 11: Final Project (Industry Project), Project II presentations
resources: Course notes (Learn)
M. Morris Mano, Digital Design, Prentice Hall Int. Inc., 2th Edition, 1991, ISBN: 81-203-0835-2.
Digital Design, M. Morris Mano, Michael D. Ciletti, Prentice Hall Int. Inc., 4th Edition, 2007, ISBN: 0-13-198924-3.
Digital Design with RTL Design, VHDL & Verilog, Frank Vahid, Wiley, Second Edition, 2011, ISBN: 978-0-470-53108-2.
Lojik Devre Tasarımı, Taner Arsan, Rifat Çölkesen, Papatya Yayıncılık, 2.Baskı, 2007, ISBN: 978-975-6797-07-5.

courses.name: Fundamentals of Electronic Circuits
courses.purpose: To teach students the fundamentals of circuit theory and the analysis and design of simple electronic circuits using semiconductor devices, and to enable them to build and operate the related circuits in a laboratory environment.
courses.description: Basic passive and active electronic circuit elements, analysis, design, simulation, and implementation of simple circuits.
learning_outcomes: Analyze electrical circuits.
Understand the operating principles of commonly used semiconductor circuit elements.
Analyze and design simple electronic circuits using these elements and build and operate them in the laboratory.
Understand the operating principles of simple electronic devices.
weekly_topics: Hafta 3: Circuit analysis techniques, resistor circuits
Hafta 5: Capacitance, inductance and transient analysis
Hafta 6: Capacitance, inductance and transient analysis
resources: Hambley, A.R., Electrical Engineering: Principles & Applications, Global Edition, Pearson Education Limited, ISBN: 129222312X
Irwin, J.D. Nelms, R.M., Patnaik, A., Engineering Circuit Analysis, International Student Edition, John Wiley & Sons, ISBN: 9781118960639

courses.name: Mühendislik Matematiği III
courses.purpose: Bu dersin amacı çok değişkenli fonksiyonlar, çok katlı integraller ve vektör alanları konularında öğrenciyi ayrıntılı biçimde bilgilendirmek; limit, süreklilik, türev ve integral kavramlarını bu bağlamda uygulama becerisi kazandırmaktır. Ayrıca eğrisel integraller, Green teoremi, yüzey integralleri, Stokes ve Diverjans teoremlerinin öğretilmesi hedeflenir.
courses.description: Çok değişkenli fonksiyonlar ve türevleri; limit, süreklilik, kısmi türevler, zincir kuralı, doğrultu türevi, gradyent, teğet düzlem, diferansiyeller, ekstremum değerleri, Lagrange çarpanları ve Taylor formülü. Çok katlı integraller; iki katlı integraller, alanlar, kutupsal koordinatlarda iki katlı integraller, Kartezyen, silindirik ve küresel koordinatlarda üç katlı integraller, değişken dönüşümü. Vektör alanlarında integral; eğrisel integraller, iş, sirkülasyon, akı, potansiyel fonksiyonlar, korunumlu vektör alanları, Green teoremi, yüzey alanı ve yüzey integralleri, parametrik yüzeyler, Stokes teoremi ve Diverjans teoremi.
learning_outcomes: Çok değişkenli fonksiyonlarda limit ve süreklilik kavramlarını kullanabilme
Çok değişkenli fonksiyonlarda kısmi türevleri hesaplayabilme
Teğet düzlem, doğrultuya göre türev ve gradiyent bulabilme
Ekstremum problemlerini ikinci türev testi ve Lagrange çarpanlar metodu ile çözebilme
Çok katlı integralleri Kartezyen ve diğer koordinat takımlarında hesaplayabilme
Çok katlı integraller yardımı ile alan ve hacim hesaplarını yapabilme
Eğrisel integral hesaplayabilme ve Green teoremini uygulayabilme
Potansiyel fonksiyon bulabilme ve yüzey alanı hesaplayabilme
Stokes ve Diverjans teoremlerini uygulayabilme
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Çok Değişkenli Fonksiyonlar: Tanım ve Değer Kümeler
Hafta 4: Doğrultu Türevi, Gradiyent Vektör, Teğet Düzlem
Hafta 5: Normal Doğru, Diferansiyeller, Ekstremum Değerleri ve Eğer Noktaları
Hafta 6: Ekstremum Değerleri ve Eğer Noktaları
Hafta 8: Taylor`s Formula, Iki Kat Integraller
Hafta 12: Katlı Integrallerde Değişken Dönüşümü, Eğrisel Integraller
resources: Thomas` Calculus, 11e, G.B. Thomas, M.D.Weir, J.Hass and F.R.Giordano, Addison-Wesley, 2005.

courses.name: Modern Türkiye Tarihi I
courses.purpose: Bu derste Osmanlı dönemindeki modernleşme süreci ile modern Türkiye tarihi arasında ilişki kurularak modernleşme sürecindeki temel tema ve olayların tanıtılması amaçlanmaktadır. Öğrencilerin arşiv, görsel ve kurmaca kaynaklar gibi çeşitli kaynaklardan Türkiye’nin başlıca meseleleri hakkında farklı bakış açılarını öğrenmeleri ve eleştirel, tarihsel bir perspektif geliştirmeleri hedeflenmektedir.
courses.description: Bu ders Osmanlı İmparatorluğu’nun modernleşme sürecine odaklanmakta ve bu dönemde yaşanan dönüşümlerin modern Türkiye’nin oluşumuna yansımalarını incelemektedir. Ders, kronolojik bir akışla birlikte tematik modüller üzerinden ilerleyerek devlet aygıtının modernleşmesi, küresel ekonomiye eklemlenme, şehirlerin dönüşümü, modern sanat formlarının ortaya çıkışı ve toplumsal hayattaki değişiklikleri ele almaktadır. Ayrıca Osmanlı’nın son dönemindeki siyasi reformlar, siyasi ve entelektüel figürler, toplumsal yapıdaki değişimler, şehirlerin demografik yapısı, kültürel hayat ve savaş dönemlerinin değişimi tetikleyen etkileri birincil ve ikincil kaynaklar aracılığıyla işlenmektedir.
learning_outcomes: Türkiye modernleşmesiyle ilişkili olarak, Osmanlı’nın son dönemindeki önemli olaylar ve gelişmeler hakkında bilgi sahibi olmak.
Tarihsel ve eleştirel bakış açısı geliştirmek.

Arşiv, görsel ve kurgusal olanlar da dahil olmak üzere çeşitli kaynakları eleştirel bir biçimde değerlendirebilmek ve tartışabilmek, bu bilgileri çeşitli şekillerde ve farklı bakış açılarıyla yorumlayabilmek.
Yazılı ve sözlü iletişim becerilerini geliştirmek.
weekly_topics: Hafta 6: Modül III – Şehirler ve şehir hayatı
Hafta 7: Modül III – Şehirler ve şehir hayatı
Hafta 8: Modül IV – Sosyal ve Kültürel Hayat / Yeni Toplum
Hafta 9: Modül IV – Sosyal ve Kültürel Hayat / Yeni Toplum
Hafta 10: Modül V – Sosyal ve Kültürel Hayat / Sanat ve Spor Alanı
Hafta 11: Modül V – Sosyal ve Kültürel Hayat / Sanat ve Spor Alanı
Hafta 12: Modül VI – Savaş ve Değişim
Hafta 13: Modül VI – Savaş ve Değişim
resources: Her hafta için dersi destekleyen akademik makaleler, kitap bölümleri ve birincil kaynaklar belirlenmiştir.
Öğrencilerin öğretim üyesi ile birlikte seçip tartışacakları birincil kaynakların olduğu bir havuz vardır.
Akademik makaleler, kitap bölümleri ve popüler kültürel materyallerden oluşan bir önerilen kaynaklar listesi hazırlanmıştır.

courses.name: Data Structures and Algorithms
courses.purpose: The main purpose of this course is to provide students with the necessary background on the fundamentals of problem solving and to develop skills in the design and implementation of discrete data structures and algorithms widely used in computer science and computational problems.
courses.description: Introduction to data structures and algorithms. Overview of asymptotic notation and analysis tools. Recursion and its application to problem solving in computer science. Design and implementation of important abstract data types such as linked lists, doubly linked lists, stacks, and queues. Design and implementation of efficient sorting and searching algorithms and discrete search structures such as binary search trees.
learning_outcomes: analyze algorithms or computer code for correctness
evaluate algorithms and computer code in terms of efficiency
learn the most important data structures
learn how the most important data structures are implemented
learn how to use data structures to solve problems
learn some advanced algorithms
improve problem-solving skills
learn how to solve problems without using a brute-force approach
be (almost) ready for a coding interview
weekly_topics: Hafta 3: Static and Dynamic Arrays, STL
Hafta 8: Graphs, Trees, Binary Trees, Cross Trees (1)
Hafta 9: Graphs, Trees, Binary Trees, Cross Trees (2)
Hafta 13: Suffix Arrays and LCP Arrays (Temporary)
Hafta 14: Best Practices and Summary for the Final Exam
resources: M. A. Weiss, Data Structures and Algorithm Analysis in C++, Pearson, 2014, 4th Ed.
Data Structures Using C++, 2nd Edition by D. D. Malik

courses.name: Computer Organization and Architecture
courses.purpose: This course aims to build a solid foundation in computer design and implementation. It also teaches how to design an assembly instruction set and enables students to understand and design core computer components such as the bus, memory organization, control unit, and input-output structure.
courses.description: This course provides students with the basic knowledge needed to understand how computer hardware works. Main topics include an introduction to the digital blocks used in computer organization and design, basic computer design, the introduction of the microprogram concept, and the use of ASM diagrams for sequencing and controlling operations.
learning_outcomes: Build a solid foundation for computer design and development.
Build a solid foundation for computer architecture and the arithmetic logic unit.
Learn how microprogramming and the assembly language instruction set are designed for a computer system.
Learn the fundamentals of computer bus, memory organization, controller, and input-output structure.
weekly_topics: Hafta 6: Data transfer between registers and microinstructions
Hafta 7: Registers in computers and instruction sets
Hafta 8: Basic Computer Organization and Design
Hafta 9: Basic Computer Organization and Design
Hafta 11: Basic Computer Design, Accumulator Logic Design
Hafta 14: Microprogrammed Control: Control Memory, Address Sequencing, Control Unit Design
resources: M. Morris Mano, Computer System Architecture, 3. ed., Prentice Hall International, ISBN 0-13-175738-5, 1993.

courses.name: Staj I
courses.purpose: Bu dersin amacı, ikinci sınıf mühendislik öğrencilerini gerçek hayattaki iş ve istihdam koşullarına dahil ederek, onlara uygulamaya yönelik deneyim kazandırmak ve Bilgisayar mühendisliği lisans programında edindikleri bilgi ve becerilerle mesleki ortamda toplam 40 iş günü/8 hafta pratik yapmalarına olanak sağlamaktır.
courses.description: Firma tarafından verilen sorumluluklar ve iş yükleri.
learning_outcomes: Gerçek hayattaki bilişim sistemleri ve yazılım geliştirme hakkında teorik ve pratik mühendislik bilgilerinden yararlanmak.
Gerçek hayattaki mühendislik problemlerini tanımlamak.
Profesyonel bir çalışma ortamında organizasyon hiyerarşisini ve iletişimini değerlendirebilmek.
Son teslim tarihi gereksinimlerine uygun olacak şekilde grup çalışmalarında ve uygulamalarında yer almayı öğrenmek.
resources: Firma tarafından verilen iş güvenliği yönergeleri, saha yönetmelikleri, ofis yönetmelikleri, teknik kılavuzlar ve el kitapları vb. dökümanlar.
my.khas.edu.tr
fens.khas.edu.tr/zorunlu-staj/

courses.name: Numerical Systems
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to the mathematics, logic, and language of numerical methods as used in engineering and basic sciences. Students will learn how numerical methods are applied to important problems in science and industry.
courses.description: Description of numerical methods and their applications in engineering; error analysis of numerical methods and analytical solutions; numerical methods for solving systems of linear and nonlinear equations; approximation methods, interpolation, linear regression, and numerical integration; numerical methods for solving differential equations.
learning_outcomes: Understand the fundamentals of numerical methods.
Use knowledge of numerical methods while solving a problem.
Select the appropriate numerical method to apply in a given situation.
Present projects using numerical methods orally and in writing.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction, decimal numbers, accuracy, stability concepts. Solutions of nonlinear equations in one variable. Bisection method, Newton-Raphson method.
Hafta 2: Introduction to Matlab. Applications from various engineering fields. Solution and error analysis in Matlab.
Hafta 3: Presentation and discussion of equation solving. Submission of project report, presentation and feedback.
Hafta 4: Direct and iterative solutions of linear equation systems, eigenvalues, eigenvectors.
Hafta 5: Application from various disciplines using Matlab libraries. Assignment of the project.
Hafta 6: Project presentations and discussions. Report writing, submission, presentation and feedback.
Hafta 8: Least squares regression method. Newton interpolation and Lagrange polynomials and interpolation. Fourier method and signal analysis.
Hafta 9: Matlab applications related to numerical integration and differentiation. In-class calculations. Project introduction.
Hafta 10: Project presentations and discussions. Report writing, submission, presentation and feedback.
Hafta 11: Solution of differential equations with Euler and Heun methods. Initial value problems and Runge-Kutta method.
Hafta 12: Application from various disciplines using Matlab libraries. Project introduction.
Hafta 13: Project presentations and discussions. Report writing, submission, presentation and feedback.
resources: Numerical Methods for Engineers, Chapra and Canale, 7th Edition, McGraw Hill.

courses.name: Modern Türkiye Tarihi II
courses.purpose: Bu dersin temel amacı öğrencilere modern Türkiye tarihindeki başlıca tema ve olayların tanıtılmasıdır. Öğrenciler arşiv, görsel ve kurmaca kaynaklar gibi çeşitli kaynaklardan Türkiye’nin başlıca meseleleri hakkında farklı bakış açılarını öğrenecekler. Ders öğrencilere Türkiye’yi küresel bir bağlama oturtma ve Türkiye tarihinin çoğulcu doğasını fark etmelerinde yardımcı olacaktır. Ders öğrencileri bilgili ve sorgulayıcı vatandaşlar yapmayı hedeflemektedir.
courses.description: Bu ders, erken Cumhuriyet döneminden günümüze kadar modern Türkiye tarihini siyasi, sosyal, ekonomik ve kültürel yönleriyle incelemektedir. Ders, belirli temalara dayalı modül temelli bir yapının ardından, Türkiye tarihinin genel eğilimleri ve süreçlerindeki kırılmalar ve sürekliliklere odaklanır. Altı modül boyunca, imparatorluktan cumhuriyete, tek partili sistemden çok partili sisteme geçiş, Soğuk Savaş'tan yeni küresel dünyaya geçiş gibi konular köyden kente göç, toplumsal hareketler, neo-liberalleşme, siyasal İslam ve kimlik siyasetiyle ilişkili çeşitli sosyal ve ekonomi kültürel yönleriyle tartışılacaktır. Bu bağlamda, ders aslında üç ilke üzerine planlanmıştır: İlk olarak Türkiye'yi küresel bir bağlama yerleştirir, dolayısıyla bağlantıları ve kopuklukları vurgular. İkinci olarak hem devlet düzeyindeki dönüşümleri hem de “sıradan” insanların bu dönüşümlerden nasıl etkilendiğini değerlendirir. Son olarak ise, ikincil rehber kaynakların yanı sıra, birincil kaynakları kullanarak öğrencilerin geçmişle kişisel bir ilişki kurmalarına yardımcı olur ve onların tarihi bir dizi olay yerine daha derin bir şekilde anlamalarını teşvik eder.
learning_outcomes: Türkiye'nin modern tarihinin önemli olayları ve sorunları hakkında bilgi edinmek, “öteki” yaşamlar hakkında fikir edinmek, modern Türkiye tarihini küresel bağlamda konumlandırmak
Türkiye tarihini farklı kaynaklardan farklı bakış açılarıyla yorumlayabilmek

Arşiv, görsel ve kurgusal olanlar da dahil olmak üzere çeşitli kaynakları eleştirel bir biçimde değerlendirebilmek ve tartışabilmek, bu bilgileri çeşitli şekillerde ve farklı bakış açılarıyla yorumlayabilmek ve sunabilmek
Kişisel deneyimler ile Türkiye'deki siyasi ve sosyal dönüşümlerle bütünleştirebilmek
Yazılı ve sözlü iletişim becerilerini hem takım hem de bireysel çalışmalarla geliştirmek
resources: Her hafta için dersi destekleyen akademik makaleler, kitap bölümleri ve birincil kaynaklar belirlenmiştir.
Öğrencilerin öğretim üyesi ile birlikte seçip tartışacakları birincil kaynakların olduğu bir havuz vardır.
Akademik makaleler, kitap bölümleri ve popüler kültürel materyallerden oluşan bir önerilen kaynaklar listesi hazırlanmıştır.

courses.name: Object-Oriented Programming Languages
courses.purpose: This course aims to improve students' understanding of the fundamental concepts of object-oriented programming. It covers core and advanced object-oriented concepts such as classes, objects, inheritance, polymorphism, and encapsulation, and aims to demonstrate these concepts through the development of solutions and projects using Java.
courses.description: Introduction to Object-Oriented Programming. Advanced Object-Oriented Programming Concepts. Exception Handling. Concurrency. GUI Development. Files and Streams.
learning_outcomes: Understand and use object-oriented programming concepts and various data structures.
Use the Java programming language to develop applications of varying complexity.
Create problem solutions through Java and various algorithms and design graphical user interfaces (GUI).
Participate in a software development team and apply appropriate presentation skills.
weekly_topics: Hafta 10: Object-Oriented Design and UML, Exception Handling
resources: Java: How to Program, Late Objects, by Paul Deitel & Harvey Deitel, 11th Edition, Pearson.
Intro to Java Programming, Comprehensive Version, by Y. Daniel Liang, 10th Edition, Pearson.

courses.name: Computer Networks
courses.purpose: The main purpose of this course is to examine the fundamental principles, protocols, and various applications in the design and implementation of computer networks. By the end of the course, students are expected to understand layered network architecture, design issues, and the protocols used at each layer, and to identify cyber security threats and propose solutions to them.
courses.description: Overview of computer networks. Network architecture and the OSI model. Network topology, link analysis, delay analysis. Physical layer, transmission and multiplexing, terminal management, errors. Data link layer and link protocols. Network layer, routing and congestion, satellite and packet radio networks, local area networks. Transport and session layer, presentation layer, application layer. Main topics of cyber security.
learning_outcomes: Understand and use layered network architectures (OSI, TCP/IP, IP stack) and related protocols.
Gain the ability to effectively use Wireshark to capture, analyze, and interpret network traffic packets.
Understand the importance of security in computer networks.
Join a network engineering team to design and implement various computer network solutions and effectively present proposed solutions using presentation techniques.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Computer Networks and the Internet
Hafta 2: Computer Networks and the Internet
Hafta 10: Link Layer and Local Area Networks
Hafta 11: Link Layer and Local Area Networks
resources: Computer Networking, A Top-Down Approach, 7th Edition, James F. Kurose, Keith W. Ross, Pearson Education Inc.
Computer Networks, 5th Edition by Andrew S. Tanenbaum, David W. Wetherall, Pearson Education Inc.
Communication Networks: A First Course, 2nd Edition by Jean Walrand, McGraw-Hill.
Data Networks, 2nd Edition by Dimitri Bertsekas & Robert Gallager, Prentice Hall.

courses.name: Probability and Statistics
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to probability and statistics theory and applications, and to provide the basic knowledge needed for data analysis in engineering systems.
courses.description: Module 1 covers data presentation and analysis, probability concepts and axioms, random variables, and mathematical expectations. Module 2 includes discrete and continuous probability distributions, probability calculations, joint distributions, conditional probability, and independence. Module 3 addresses probability distributions, estimation and confidence intervals, and hypothesis testing. Module 4 introduces experimental design. Module 5 presents the concepts of risk and reliability.
learning_outcomes: Calculate statistics of numerical data.
Use probability concepts and random variables to represent uncertainty in events.
Determine statistics of random variables.
Solve probability calculation problems involving probability density and cumulative distribution functions.
Recognize several common probability distributions and solve distribution problems.
Be familiar with applications of probability and statistics.
Perform experimental design, reliability analysis, and risk analysis.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to statistics. Mean and deviation. Probability and counting rules. Conditional probability and Bayes' rule. In-class lecture.
Hafta 2: Quality of life experiment application, in-class calculations at the end of the lesson. Excel application. Project introduction.
Hafta 3: Presentation and discussion of the introduction to statistics and probability project. Report writing and presentation.
Hafta 4: Random variables, probability distributions. Observations on binomial, normal, and Poisson distributions. In-class lecture.
Hafta 5: Box counting experiment application. Sampling distribution and confidence interval. Control charts project assigned as homework.
Hafta 6: Presentation of the control charts project. Report writing, presentation, and discussion.
Hafta 8: Experimental design in-class lecture. Model building and testing. Calculations with Excel.
Hafta 9: Designing and testing a home-school route experiment. Application with Excel. Project introduction and assignment.
Hafta 10: Presentation of the experimental design project and report preparation.
Hafta 11: Reliability and risk analysis in-class lecture. First-order reliability analysis. Examination of systems.
Hafta 12: Applications from electrical, computer, civil, and mechatronics systems. Project introduction.
Hafta 13: Reliability and risk analysis project presentations. Report preparation and presentation.
resources: Walpole, R.E., R.H. Meyers, S.L Meyers, and K. Ye, Probability and Statistics for Engineers and Scientists, 9th Ed. Pearson International Edition, Prentice-Hall, 2012.

courses.name: Graph Theory
courses.purpose: The main purpose of this course is to enable students to learn and use classical theorems and algorithms in algorithmic graph theory. Students are expected to demonstrate their algorithm knowledge by solving some basic practical graph problems. They will also learn several applications of graph algorithms in computer engineering and be able to formulate engineering problems on graphs and develop algorithms for them.
courses.description: This course discusses the elements of graph theory with emphasis on graph algorithms. About half of the course is devoted to graph-theoretic topics and the other half to algorithmic applications. Topics include minimum spanning trees, Euler circuits, graph coloring, matchings, connectivity, and Hamiltonian circuits. In addition, some advanced topics such as list coloring and combinatorial games are also covered.
learning_outcomes: Define and use basic concepts related to graphs.
Relate graph invariants to practical examples.
Use the foundations of graph theory to prove results, understand and use graph structures, and compute graph invariants for certain graph classes.
Use algorithmic techniques to work efficiently with graph parameters and properties.
Design efficient algorithms for many optimization problems defined on graphs.
Apply techniques used in graph theory effectively to engineering topics such as chip design and communication networks.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Fundamentals of graph theory. Applications of graphs (telecommunication networks and similar).
Hafta 2: Some special graph families. Euler circuits. Degree sequences. Havel-Hakimi algorithm. Isomorphic graphs.
Hafta 3: Computer representation of graphs. DFS and BFS algorithms. First quiz.
Hafta 4: Minimum spanning trees. Kruskal and Prim algorithms.
Hafta 5: Matching problems and applications. Hall's theorem. Shortest path algorithms.
Hafta 6: Connectivity. Strongly connected components. Second quiz.
Hafta 7: Maximum flow problems. Cliques, vertex cover, independent sets.
Hafta 8: Graph chromatic number, upper bounds, Brook's theorem.
Hafta 9: Graph coloring problem and related algorithms. Midterm exam.
Hafta 12: Introduction to computational complexity. Examples of NP-complete problems defined on graphs (Longest path, Hamiltonian cycle). Third quiz.
Hafta 13: Ramsey theorem. List coloring problem and applications.
Hafta 14: Combinatorial games on graphs. Edge search problem and its variations in engineering applications.
resources: Douglas West, Introduction to Graph Theory, 2001, 2nd ed.
Alan Gibbons, Algorithmic Graph Theory, 1985.
Cormen, Leiserson, Rivest, Stein, Introduction to Algorithms, 2009, 3rd ed.
Martin Charles Golumbic, Algorithmic Graph Theory and Perfect Graphs, 2004.

courses.name: Formal Languages and Automata Theory
courses.purpose: This course emphasizes three concepts: what computation is, what can be computed with which computational model, and what cannot be computed. Since the course topics are mathematical in nature, proofs are presented throughout the course. Developing reasoning and proof skills in computational problems is one of the main aims of the course.
courses.description: Introduction to basic concepts of computation. After establishing the relationship between computational problems and languages, languages (regular languages, context-free languages, etc.) are classified according to computational complexity and the corresponding computational model for each class is introduced. Each computational model is represented by a corresponding automaton (machine).
learning_outcomes: Understand the concept of abstract machines and their power to recognize languages.
Use finite-state machines to model and solve computational problems.
Design context-free grammars for formal languages.
Gain competence in mathematical tools and formal methods.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Overview of automata, computability, and complexity. Mathematical concepts and terminology.
Hafta 2: Deterministic finite automata. Regular expressions.
Hafta 3: Nondeterministic finite automata. Equivalence of NFA and DFA.
Hafta 4: Formal definition of regular expressions. Equivalence in finite automata.
Hafta 6: Review and evaluation of first midterm exam questions. Non-regular languages. Pumping lemmas for regular languages.
Hafta 8: Definition of context-free grammars (CFG) and context-free languages.
Hafta 10: Definition of pushdown automata (PDA). PDA examples.
Hafta 12: Review and evaluation of second midterm exam questions. Equivalence of PDAs and CFGs.
Hafta 14: Definition of Turing machines (TM). TM examples.
resources: Introduction to the Theory of Computation, 3rd edition (2nd edition also accepted), Michael Sipser, Cengage Learning, 2013.
Introduction to Automata Theory, Languages, and Computation, 3rd Edition, John E. Hopcroft, Rajeev Motwani, Jeffrey D. Ullman, Pearson, 2007.
Course notes

courses.name: Software Engineering Project
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with the fundamental principles of software system development process and object-oriented technology, and to help them understand how to develop a software system from scratch. It also enables students to experience different software process models, software requirements engineering, project management, system analysis, design, implementation, verification, documentation, and presentation.
courses.description: This course covers the software development process, requirements elicitation and analysis, specification, design, implementation, testing, and evolution. It introduces software process models, software requirements, project management, human management, system models, architectural design, implementation, verification, and various concepts, techniques, and tools for software development. Student groups apply the topics learned in this course by developing a real-life project.
learning_outcomes: Recognize software development process models.
Analyze, design, and develop a software system.
Perform software testing and prepare software development project documentation.
Participate in a software development team, design and implement a web-based automation system, and use project management steps with proper presentation skills.
weekly_topics: Hafta 3: Angular, React, Spring and Spring Boot tools.
resources: Software Engineering, 10th Edition, Ian Sommerville, Pearson, 2016
Object-Oriented Software Engineering Using UML, Patterns, and Java, 3rd Edition. Bernd Bruegge, Allen H. Dutoit, Pearson, 2010.

courses.name: Staj II
courses.purpose: Bu dersin amacı, üçüncü sınıf mühendislik öğrencilerini gerçek hayattaki iş ve istihdam koşullarına dahil ederek, onlara uygulamaya yönelik deneyim kazandırmak ve Bilgisayar mühendisliği lisans programında edindikleri bilgi ve becerilerle mesleki ortamda toplam 40 iş günü/8 hafta pratik yapmalarına olanak sağlamaktır.
courses.description: Firma tarafından verilen sorumluluklar ve iş yükleri.
learning_outcomes: Gerçek hayattaki bilişim sistemleri ve yazılım geliştirme hakkında teorik ve pratik mühendislik bilgilerinden yararlanmak.
Gerçek hayattaki mühendislik problemlerini tanımlamak.
Profesyonel bir çalışma ortamında organizasyon hiyerarşisini ve iletişimini değerlendirebilmek.
Son teslim tarihi gereksinimlerine uygun olacak şekilde grup çalışmalarında ve uygulamalarında yer almayı öğrenmek.
resources: Firma tarafından verilen iş güvenliği yönergeleri, saha yönetmelikleri, ofis yönetmelikleri, teknik kılavuzlar ve el kitapları vb. dökümanlar.

courses.name: Engineering Design Project I
courses.purpose: The aim of this course is to introduce engineering students to the nature and definition of the engineering problem-solving process and to present the theory and applications of commonly used methods. It also aims to cover project management and related areas of work and show their benefits for problem solving.
courses.description: Introduction to the engineering world, block diagrams and design, fundamentals of engineering design, process development and reporting, quality tools and methods (fishbone, Pareto, control charts, histograms, cause-and-effect diagrams, flowcharts, etc.), project management and engineering, problem-solving methods, research methods, and introduction to the engineering design project.
learning_outcomes: To be able to define and classify engineering problems
To be able to define the stages of engineering design and use design techniques in these stages
To be able to participate in multidisciplinary team work, write technical reports, and make presentations
To be able to apply problem-solving techniques
To be able to design the project and risk management process
To be able to carry out the conceptual and functional design stages for a complex engineering problem
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to the engineering world
Hafta 3: Engineering Design, Conceptual Design, Preliminary Design, Detailed Design
Hafta 7: Human as a Social Being in Team Work and Team Work
Hafta 11: Project Management: How to Create a Project Proposal?
Hafta 12: Introduction to the engineering design project
Hafta 13: Project Management: How to Write a Project?
resources: Seyyed Khandani, Engineering Design Process, available at: http://www.saylor.org/me101
Messler, Robert, Engineering Problem-Solving 101: Time-Tested and Timeless Techniques, McGraw Hill, 2013

courses.name: Engineering Design Project II
courses.purpose: Students use the knowledge and skills they have acquired during their undergraduate education to develop a solution to an open-ended, realistic engineering problem and realize it as a product.
courses.description: This is a design project that constitutes the final stage of undergraduate education for senior students. An interdisciplinary project is carried out by a team of 2-4 students under the supervision of one or more faculty members.
learning_outcomes: Ability to define a complex engineering problem, determine an analysis method for its solution, and design the system or product to be developed
Ability to use appropriate tools for the analysis and solution of a complex problem and to collect and process the necessary data
Ability to actively participate in project work, write technical reports, and present them
Ability to follow developments in science and technology while solving a complex problem, and awareness of acting in accordance with standards and ethical principles
Ability to design and implement project management and risk management plans during the project, and awareness of health, environment, and safety issues
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Submission of the project proposal to the advisor
Hafta 6: Submission of the project progress report
Hafta 11: Submission of the project progress report to the advisor
Hafta 14: Submission of the project report and appendices to the advisor

courses.name: Entrepreneurship
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide a comprehensive guide to applied entrepreneurship. Students will work hands-on to transform an idea into a company ready for financing. By the end of the course, students are expected to gain entrepreneurial knowledge about founding, idea generation, customer validation, financing, hiring, business model development, and investor pitching.
courses.description: The course covers the fundamentals of entrepreneurship and startups, venture capital, idea generation, competition, product development, team building and execution, growth, startup finance and economics, sales and marketing in startups, and fundraising. It also includes weekly workshops on customer validation, value proposition development, business model development, and investor pitching.
learning_outcomes: Ability to explain and discuss the fundamentals of startup entrepreneurship.
Ability to find a good business idea and analyze it in terms of competition.
Ability to profile and validate customers.
Ability to model and apply startup economics.
Ability to design a value proposition.
Ability to develop a business model.
Ability to prepare investor presentations.
weekly_topics: Hafta 4: Developing a Product That Users Love / Talking to Users
Hafta 14: Investor Presentations - A Venture Seeking Investment
resources: Zero to One, Peter Thiel.
Business Model Generation, Alexander Osterwalder & Yves Pigneur.
Girişimci Kapital, Mustafa Ergen.
Essays (paulgraham.com/articles.html), Paul Graham.
Ders sunumları, değer önerisi ve iş modeli kanvasları, podcastler, girişimcilik üzerine kitaplar.

courses.name: Physical Equilibrium of Systems
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce fundamental civil engineering concepts related to equilibrium, stability, and balance. It also prepares students for other civil engineering courses by building a foundation in statics and structural behavior.
courses.description: Introduction to fundamental civil engineering concepts, force and moment concepts, vector algebra, balance and equilibrium, static and stable systems, free-body diagrams, common connection types in civil engineering, truss systems, equilibrium moment, distributed loads, and moment of inertia using first and second area integrals.
learning_outcomes: Ability to determine the direction and magnitude of forces and moments acting on physical systems.
Ability to decide whether a physical system is in equilibrium.
Ability to determine whether a physical system is static or stable.
Ability to draw free-body diagrams of physical systems using vector algebra.
Ability to identify different connection types and the resistance types formed in connections.
Ability to conduct/design experiments and observations and present these observations.
Ability to understand the concept of moment of inertia and calculate the moments of inertia of different cross-sections using the second area integral.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Module 1 Force: Bridge design with spaghetti. Loading of bridges and understanding the concept of force through the forces acting on the bridge. Introduction to vector algebra.
Hafta 2: Module 2 Moment: Understanding the concept of moment through experiments. In-class moment calculations at the end of the lesson.
Hafta 3: Module 3 Equality and Equilibrium: Understanding and discussing equilibrium, static and stable concepts through experiments. In-class calculations.

Hafta 4: Module 3 Equality and Equilibrium: Understanding free-body diagrams through experiments. Drawing free-body diagrams in class. Project: Drawing the free-body diagram of the Eiffel Tower. Report writing and presentation preparation.

Hafta 5: Module 3 Equality and Equilibrium: Observing different connection and joint types in the laboratory. Understanding resistance types in connections and calculating resistances. Demonstrating internal forces acting on a beam through experiments.

Hafta 6: Module 3 Equality and Equilibrium: Observing truss systems and solving these systems. Mini project: Photographing civil engineering applications around you and naming them. Observations and predictions about how these systems work. Short report writing.
Hafta 7: Module 4 Area Integral: Observing distributed loads. In-class calculations, converting distributed loads to point loads using the first area integral.
Hafta 8: Module 4 Area Integral: Understanding the concept of moment of inertia through experiments. Calculating moment of inertia using the second area integral.
Hafta 9: Project: Calculating/comparing the moment of inertia of different cross-sections. Report writing, presentation, and exchange of ideas.
Hafta 10: On-site observation of load transfer mechanisms. Discussions, technical trip, and preparation of trip report.
Hafta 11: Repetition of the spaghetti experiment from the first week with the knowledge gained during the year. Calculation of loads acting on the bridge / structural analysis of the bridge.
Hafta 12: Detailed reporting of the bridge project, presentations, and in-class discussions.
resources: Engineering Mechanics Statics by Hibbeler
University Physics by Young & Freedman

courses.name: Litosfer
courses.purpose: Bu ders, yaşama olanak sağlayan ve yaşamın devam etmesine destek veren yer kabuğunun çeşitli yönlerini incelemeyi amaçlamaktadır.
courses.description: Ders; litosferin yapılı çevreye desteği, kayaç ve zeminlerin kompozisyonu, plaka tektoniği, erozyon, yeraltı suyu ve enerji kaynakları gibi konuları proje temelli olarak ele almaktadır. Her modülde giriş, sınıf içi tartışma, uygulama/ gözlem, sunum ve rapor çalışmaları yer almaktadır.
learning_outcomes: Yazılı ve sözlü olarak etkili iletişim kurabilecektir.
Analiz yapabilme ve kararlara ulaşmak için ekiplerde etkin ve saygılı bir şekilde çalışabilme yeteneğini geliştirecektir.
Doğal süreçlerin önemini ve çevreyi şekillendirme ve değiştirme şeklini tanımlayabilecektir.
Doğal süreçlerin ve zeminin inşaat mühendisliği projeleri üzerindeki bazı etkilerini vurgulayabilecektir.
Litosferdeki çeşitli enerji kaynakları hakkında bilgi sahibi olacak ve bu kaynakların nasıl çıkarıldığının farkında olacaktır.
İnsanların faaliyetlerinin dünya yüzeyinin sürdürülebilirliğini nasıl etkilediği hakkında farkında olacaktır.
weekly_topics: Hafta 3: Proje 2 Litosferin Kompozisyonu: Kayalar ve Zemin
Hafta 4: Proje 2 Litosferin Kompozisyonu: Kayalar ve Zemin (devam)
Hafta 14: Ders konularının tekrarı ve özeti

Foundations of Engineering Geology, by T. Waltham, 3rd Edition, Taylor&Francis

courses.name: Topography
courses.purpose: This course aims to equip students with the ability to apply mathematics, science, and engineering knowledge to understand surveying techniques and equipment used in land measurement. It also introduces terrestrial and satellite-based surveying methods and tools, and helps students understand both the theory and practice of land surveying.
courses.description: Measurement, measurement errors, positioning methods and systems; terrestrial surveying methods and instruments; satellite-based surveying methods (GNSS and remote sensing); geographic information systems (GIS), photogrammetric surveying methods, deformation measurements; height determination, leveling, surface leveling, profile and cross-section leveling; profile and cross-section leveling calculations, area and volume calculations; properties of contour lines and classical map use.
learning_outcomes: Explains the processes from engineering projects to the physical earth surface using reference system concepts.
Defines measurement errors and calculates errors in measurement observations.
Knows terrestrial and satellite-based surveying instruments and explains where they are used.
Understands the use of 3D positions and deformation measurements in engineering and calculates coordinate and elevation values.
Explains GNSS and remote sensing concepts and plans their use in various applications.
Defines GIS and photogrammetry concepts and plans their use in various applications.
Calculates land area and volume values in engineering projects using profiles, cross-sections, and various methods.

Hafta 4: Terrestrial and satellite-based surveying instruments
Hafta 5: Terrestrial and satellite-based surveying instruments
Hafta 6: Project 2: Surveying application with surveying equipment
Hafta 7: Geographic Information Systems (GIS), photogrammetric surveying methods, deformation measurements
Hafta 8: Geographic Information Systems (GIS), photogrammetric surveying methods, deformation measurements
Hafta 9: Project 3: Application of GIS methods

Hafta 12: Properties of contour lines, classical map use
Hafta 13: Project 4: Area and volume calculations
