courses.name: Investigation of Fluid Behavior
courses.purpose: This course aims to discuss the fundamental principles of fluid mechanics and apply these principles to the liquid form of water.
courses.description: The course covers fluid properties, hydrostatics, fluid kinematics, analysis methods, fluid dynamics, continuity, momentum and energy equations and their applications, dimensional analysis, similarity and modeling. It is organized in five modules with lectures, applications/experiments, and project presentations.
learning_outcomes: Perform hydrostatic calculations related to fluids.
Calculate velocity, acceleration, and flow curves in fluid kinematics.
Establish continuity, momentum, and energy equations using the control volume method.
Identify dimensionless quantities that define a problem using dimensional analysis.
Design experiments.

courses.name: Structural Mechanics
courses.purpose: This course aims to investigate structural theory and the principles of structural analysis. It introduces the fundamental concepts needed to understand how loads are transferred and how structural systems behave.
courses.description: The course covers linear elasticity theory of structures, determinate and indeterminate systems, slope and deflection equations, principles of structural analysis using stiffness and flexibility approaches, sketching of bending shapes, structural analysis programs, and load transfer mechanisms.
learning_outcomes: Gain the ability to identify and distinguish the load transfer methods of basic structural elements such as trusses, beams, columns, disks, and shells under horizontal and vertical forces.
Define how loads are transferred in structures, for example from roof to foundation, and develop an understanding of load transfer mechanisms.
Identify the difference between determinate and indeterminate structures.
Gain the ability to conduct experiments and observations, design based on them, present these observations, and analyze them.

courses.name: Mukavemet
courses.purpose: Yapı elemanlarında gerilme-gerinme mekanizmalarının betimlenmesi.
courses.description: Normal gerilme ve gerinme; eksenel yüklü çubuklarda gerilme ve gerinme; cıvatalı ve perçinlenmiş bağlantılarda kesme gerilmesi ve kesme gerinmesi; gerilme-gerinme ilişkisi, esneklik katsayısı ve Poisson etkisi; uyumluluk ve hiperstatik problemler; dairesel çubukların burulması; sade ve aynı eksenli eğilme; gerilme dönüşümü ve Mohr diyagramı.
learning_outcomes: Burulma altında elemanların gerilmelerinin belirlenmesi becerisi geliştirme.
İç kuvvet ve moment nedeniyle gerilmenin farklı bileşenlerinin hesaplanması yetisi kazanabilme.
Birleşik yükler altında gerilmelerin belirlenmesi yetisi kazanabilme.
Çeşitli yük tipleri altında şekil değiştirmelerin hesaplanması becerisi kazanabilme.
Deney tasarlayabilme, gözlem yapabilme, analiz edebilme, gözlemleri yazılı ve sözlü ifade edebilme.

courses.name: Physical Systems Dynamics
courses.purpose: The aim of this course is to develop an understanding of particle and rigid body dynamics as well as free and forced vibrations, and to apply these concepts to a basic structural dynamics assessment.
courses.description: Module 1: Review of the course Physical Systems Equilibrium (CIV102). Module 2: Particle dynamics. Module 3: Rigid body dynamics. Module 4: Free and forced vibrations.

Understanding rigid body dynamics and solving related numerical problems
Understanding undamped free and forced vibrations and solving the undamped free and forced vibration of a single-degree-of-freedom system

courses.name: Construction Materials Investigation
courses.purpose: To provide students with an understanding of construction materials and their behavior.
courses.description: The course covers the mechanical properties of materials such as creep, relaxation, brittleness, ductility, hardness, fatigue, toughness, flexibility, and damping; elastic and plastic behavior; viscosity; rheological models; and the properties and uses of lime, gypsum, hydraulic cement, mineral aggregates, concrete, clay materials, ferrous metals, polymers, tar-based materials, and wood.
learning_outcomes: Gain the ability to understand the production, variety, properties, and applications of construction materials in civil engineering structures.
Gain the ability to explain the relationship between macroscopic material behavior and microstructure, including the understanding of mechanical properties and durability concepts.
Gain the ability to understand the physical and mechanical properties of construction materials through standard experimental methods.
Gain awareness of material strength, durability, and cost, appropriate material selection, material design, and structural design.
Gain the ability to conduct experiments and observations and present these observations.

courses.name: Construction Site Management
courses.purpose: To understand all levels of construction site management.
courses.description: Site characteristics, organization charts, logistics lines, workflow and supply chain preparation, site setup works, responsibilities of site managers and workshops, temporary structures and storage areas, supply and installation of facilities, quality assurance/quality control and occupational health and safety requirements in construction sites, testing/commissioning and handover, project completion report, public relations, worker relations, coordination with third parties, licensing, and fee arrangements.
learning_outcomes: Understand the setup of a construction site.
Recognize the different phases of a project.
Effectively manage project documentation.
Understand the importance of public relations and worker relations.
Be able to make individual and group presentations and work in a team.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Module 1: Construction Site Management; Site characteristics
Hafta 2: Module 1: Organization charts; Preparation of logistics lines, workflow, supply chain
Hafta 3: Application of Module 1 on a real project, short presentation
Hafta 4: Module 2: Site setup works (site managers and workshops, temporary structures, storage area)
Hafta 5: Module 2: Organization chart, employee responsibilities, supply and installation of facilities
Hafta 6: Application of Module 2 on a real project, short presentation
Hafta 7: Module 3: Quality assurance/quality control and occupational health and safety in construction sites today
Hafta 9: Application of Module 3 on a real project, short presentation
Hafta 10: Module 4: Project completion report; Public relations
Hafta 11: Module 4: Worker relations; Coordination with third parties, licensing, fee arrangements
Hafta 12: Application of Module 4 on a real project, short presentation

courses.name: Transportation Engineering
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce and teach the fundamental components of transportation engineering, with a focus on the planning and design of highway and railway facilities.
courses.description: Project 1 Planning: Survey and field study. Project 2 Planning: Capacity. Project 3 Geometric Design: 2D modeling (plan, profile, sections). Project 4 Intersection and junction design. Project 5 Earthworks. Project 6 Design of highway and railway elements.
learning_outcomes: Communicate effectively in written and oral forms.
Develop the ability to analyze and work effectively and respectfully in teams to reach decisions.
Be aware of sustainable practices in transportation.
Be able to model the movement of highway and railway vehicles by conducting field studies and considering vehicle and human characteristics.
Be able to define the basic concepts related to highway alignment and the basic elements of highways and railway lines.
Be aware of the design characteristics of horizontal and vertical alignment elements of highways and railway lines.
Be aware of the design principles of highway and railway lines and be able to use their standards.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction + Planning: Survey and Field Study
Hafta 2: Project 1 Planning: Survey and Field Study
Hafta 4: Project 2 Planning: Capacity (continued) + Project 3 Geometric Design
Hafta 7: Review and evaluation of topics
Hafta 12: Project 6 Design of highway and railway elements
Hafta 13: Project 6 Design of highway and railway elements

Railway management and engineering. V. A. Profillidis, Routledge; 4th edition, 2016.

Karayolu Mühendisliği ve Tasarımı. Baha Vural Kök, Nobel Akademik Yayıncılık, 2019.

courses.name: Hydraulics
courses.purpose: This course aims to discuss the fundamental principles of hydraulics with students and then apply these principles to hydraulic problems in Civil Engineering.
courses.description: Flow in pipes and energy losses (Module 1); pipe flows and pipe networks (Module 2); open channel flows and uniform flow (Module 3); open channel flows and rapidly varied flows (Module 4); open channel flows and gradually varied flows (Module 5).
learning_outcomes: Calculate fundamental variables such as energy losses and velocity in closed and open channels
Design cross-sections in closed and open channels
Model and analyze closed and open channel problems with the help of software
Understand the fundamentals of hydraulics through experiments
Develop different solution approaches by designing hydraulic problems
Review hydraulic projects and present them orally and in written report form
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: 1A) Determination of regime and losses in closed (pressurized) flows; in-class lecture. Examination of the pipe flow experimental setup
Hafta 3: 1C) Project presentations in pressurized flows
Hafta 4: 2A) Pipe flow networks lecture and application
Hafta 5: 2B) Project presentations on pipe flow networks
Hafta 7: 3B) Project presentations and exchange of ideas
Hafta 9: 4A) Lecture on rapidly varied flows in open channels
Hafta 10: 4B) Applications in rapidly varied flows. Project introduction
Hafta 11: 4C) Project presentations, exchange of ideas
Hafta 12: 5A) Lecture on gradually varied flows and profiles in open channels
Hafta 13: 5B) Applications, project presentations and evaluation
resources: Munson, Okiishi, Young, Fundamentals of Fluid Mechanics, Wiley.

courses.name: Water Resources Engineering
courses.purpose: This course covers water resources concepts in civil engineering applications. The general aim is to understand water resources development and the required methodologies, and to gain the ability to apply mathematics, science, and engineering knowledge to solve water resources problems.
courses.description: The course covers the following main topics in detail: water resources management, hydrology, groundwater hydrology, dams and reservoirs, spillways and energy dissipation structures, diversion weirs, hydroelectric power plants, water supply and distribution, and wastewater and stormwater collection and disposal.
learning_outcomes: Explains hydrologic process concepts, defines hydrograph characteristics, analyzes rainfall data, and develops a unit hydrograph.
Collects water data and analyzes it using appropriate methods.
Discusses and explains water resources projects and identifies and solves water resources problems.
Can design basic hydraulic structures based on water resources principles.
Explains the concepts of water supply, wastewater, and stormwater collection systems.
Explains design transmission lines, gravity lines, storm sewer, and sanitary sewer systems.
Explains one of the current issues related to water resources in the format of a research essay.
weekly_topics: Hafta 4: Dams, reservoirs, and related structures
Hafta 6: Various weirs and small hydropower plants
Hafta 8: Project presentations and exchange of ideas
Hafta 11: Project presentations and exchange of ideas
Hafta 13: Applications, project presentations, and evaluation
resources: Munson, Okiishi, Young, Fundamentals of Fluid Mechanics, Wiley.

courses.name: Steel Structure Design
courses.purpose: To understand the behavior of structural steel and the basic principles of steel design.
courses.description: Structural steels and their properties; codes and regulations; tension members and trusses; connections; joints; structural fastening elements and welded connections; and non-structural design considerations.
learning_outcomes: Ability to understand the mechanical behavior of structural steel.
Ability to comprehend the behavior of steel members under tension.
Ability to size simple steel structures according to serviceability and ultimate limit states and design codes.
Ability to understand the behavior of steel connection elements such as rivets, bolts, and welds.
Ability to design, analyze, and present simple steel structures.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Phase 1 Understanding structural steels and their properties
Hafta 4: Phase 2 Determining governing loads for design
Hafta 5: Phase 2 Determining governing loads for design
Hafta 7: Phase 3 Determining loads on tension members
Hafta 10: Phase 4 Determining loads on compression members
Hafta 13: Adding non-structural issues to the design

courses.name: Soil Properties Investigation
courses.purpose: This course aims to help students understand different soil properties and engineering behavior, and how these can affect the design and construction of structures.
courses.description: The course consists of 6 modules organized as projects. The topics include soil characterization, phase relationships in soils, compaction, water in soils, consolidation settlement and consolidation time, and vertical stress in soils. Students are expected to discuss the topics in class, perform related applications and experiments, analyze results, and prepare presentations and reports for each project.
learning_outcomes: Develop the ability to communicate effectively in written and oral form.
Develop the ability to analyze and work effectively and respectfully in teams to reach decisions.
Classify soils and distinguish their general engineering properties.
Perform various laboratory tests to obtain important soil parameters.
Interpret laboratory test results.
Calculate seepage quantity, consolidation amount and time, and vertical stress in soils.
weekly_topics: Hafta 4: Project 1: Soil Classification and Project 2: Phase Relationships in Soils
Hafta 5: Project 2: Phase Relationships in Soils and Project 3: Soil Compaction
Hafta 7: Review and assessment of topics
Hafta 8: Project 3: Soil Compaction Project 4: Water in Soils
Hafta 12: Project 5: Consolidation and Project 6: Vertical Stress in Soils
Hafta 13: Project 6: Vertical Stress in Soils
resources: Robert Holtz and William D. Kovacs (2010) An Introduction to Geotechnical Engineering, 2nd edition. ISBN-10: 0132496348
Instructor's own course notes

courses.name: Temel Mühendisliği
courses.purpose: Bu ders öğrencilerin jeoloji, zemin mekaniği, kaya mekaniği ve yapı mühendisliği prensiplerini uygulayarak temel tasarım ve yapımını anlamalarına yardımcı olmayı amaçlamaktadır.
courses.description: 1. Modül 1: Saha Araştırma Teknikleri ve İdealleştirilmiş Zemin Profili 2. Modül 2: Zeminlerin Kayma Mukavemetinin Değerlendirilmesi 3. Modül 3: Temellerin Taşıma Kapasitesi 4. Modül 4: Stres Dağılımı ve Temellerin Oturması 5. Modül 5: Yanal Toprak Basıncı ve Toprak İstinat Yapıları+ Kazıkların Eksenel Yük Taşıma Kapasitesi

Analiz yapabilme ve kararlara ulaşmak için ekiplerde etkin ve saygılı bir şekilde çalışabilme yeteneğini geliştirecek
Veri toplayabilme, sonuçları yorumlayabilme ve tasarımda kullanabilme
Ortaya çıkabilecek mühendislik problemlerini tanımlayabilecek çözüm önerebilecek ve laboratuvar deneyi/saha deneyi önerebilecek
Mekanik ile ilgili bilgi birikimini mühendislik problemlerine uygulayabilme ve mühendislik tasarımı becerisi kazanacak
Mühendislikteki mesleki sorumluluğu kavrayacak

courses.name: Betonarme Elemanların Özellikleri
courses.purpose: Betonarme elemanların davranışlarının anlaşılması.
courses.description: Beton ve çeliğin mekanik özellikleri, yapısal emniyet, eksenel yükleme, basit eğilme, birleşik eğilme ve eksenel yük, birleşik kesme-eğilme-zımbalama ile burulma ve birleşik burulma-eğilme konuları işlenir. Ders, deneyler, gözlemler ve proje çalışmalarıyla betonarme elemanların davranışını ve tasarım ilkelerini ele alır.
learning_outcomes: Betonarme elemanların temel davranışlarını anlamak ve betonarme kiriş ve kolonların tasarımını yapabilme becerisi kazanma
Deneylerle betonun ve çeliğin mekanik ve fiziksel özelliklerini gözlemleme becerisi kazanma
Çeşitli beton tiplerinin karışım tasarımı hesaplamaları yapma becerisi kazanma
Deney, gözlem ve analiz yapabilme ve bu gözlemleri sunabilme yetisi kazanma
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Modül 1: Beton ve çeliğin mekanik özellikleri

courses.name: Highway Design
courses.purpose: This course aims to teach students how to design a highway by applying transportation engineering principles.
courses.description: This course covers the fundamental components of highway design. The course consists of a project divided into six phases: Phase 1: Traffic data for the design and selection of geometric standards; Phase 2: Preliminary geometric calculations and drawings; Phase 3: Route survey; Phase 4: Completion of the site survey; Phase 5: Earthworks; Phase 6: Cost analysis and project file.
learning_outcomes: Will be able to communicate effectively in written and oral form.
Will gain the ability to work effectively and respectfully in teams for analysis.
Will be able to make geometric calculations and evaluate route surveys.
Gains engineering knowledge and skills for highway alignment. Will gain engineering knowledge and skills for the design of highway infrastructure and pavement.
Will be able to perform cost calculations and economic analyses of highway routes.
Will be able to prepare technical drawings of highway design projects.
Will be able to plan the stages of a highway project and prepare a project file.
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: Brief information about traffic data for the design and selection of geometric standards (Phase 1)
Hafta 3: Traffic data for the design and selection of geometric standards (Phase 1) and introduction to preliminary geometric calculations and drawings (Phase 2)
Hafta 4: Preliminary geometric calculations and drawings (Phase 3)
Hafta 5: Preliminary geometric calculations and drawings (continued) and route survey (Phase 3)
Hafta 7: Route survey (Phase 3) + Completion of route study (Phase 4)
Hafta 8: Review and evaluation of phases
Hafta 9: Completion of route study (Phase 4) and earthworks (Phase 5)
Hafta 11: Earthworks (Phase 5) + Cost analysis and project file (Phase 6)
Hafta 12: Cost analysis and project file (Phase 6) (continued)

courses.name: Railway / Highway Design
courses.purpose: This course provides students with a comprehensive introduction to the design of railway systems. It aims to teach students how to design railway systems by applying transportation engineering principles.
courses.description: This course covers the fundamental components of railway design. The project is divided into six phases: Phase 1: Traffic data for the design and selection of geometric standards; Phase 2: Preliminary geometric calculations and drawings; Phase 3: Evaluation of the route survey; Phase 4: Completion of the site survey; Phase 5: Earthworks; Phase 6: Cost analysis and project file.
learning_outcomes: Will be able to communicate effectively in written and oral form
Will understand how to work effectively and respectfully in teams for analysis
Will be able to perform various analyses and calculations such as route analysis, geometric design, and earthworks, and use the design standards of a railway project
Will be able to make demand forecasts and select railway vehicles and trains
Will gain basic knowledge and skills about economic analysis and cost calculation methods for rail system projects
Will be able to prepare technical drawings of a railway design project
Will learn and improve the ability to prepare and present calculations
Will be able to plan the stages of a railway project and prepare a project file
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: Brief information about traffic data for the design and selection of geometric standards (Phase 1)
Hafta 3: Traffic data for the design and selection of geometric standards and introduction to route analysis and basic drawings (Phase 2)
Hafta 4: Route analysis and basic drawings (Phase 2 continuation)
Hafta 5: Route analysis and basic drawings (Phase 2) and Earthworks and Drainage (Phase 3)
Hafta 7: Earthworks and Drainage (Phase 3) (continuation) + Train operation and signaling (Phase 4)
Hafta 8: Review and evaluation of phases
Hafta 11: Track Design (Phase 5) + Cost Analysis and project file (Phase 6)
Hafta 12: Cost Analysis and project file (Phase 6) (continuation)

courses.name: Menfez Tasarımı
courses.purpose: Bu bir seçmeli tasarım dersidir.
courses.description: Aşağıdaki ana konular ayrıntılı olarak ele alınmaktadır: Hidrolojik ve saha verilerinin toplanması, hidrolik tasarım, özel uygulamalar, yapısal tasarım ve maliyet analizi.
learning_outcomes: Menfez tasarlamak için hidrolojik ve saha verilerini toplamak
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: Hidrolojik ve saha verilerinin toplanması
Hafta 13: Yenilikçi Malzemelerin Laboratuvar Testleri ve Değerlendirilmesi
resources: Munson, Okiishi, Young, Fundamentals of Fluid Mechanics, Wiley.

courses.name: Small Hydroelectric Power Plant and Water Intake Design
courses.purpose: This is an elective design course.
courses.description: Hydrological and site data collection, assessment of environmental impacts, preliminary feasibility calculations for power potential, design of components such as diversion weir and canal / pressure tunnel, head pond and penstock, turbine-generator set, draft tube, and tailrace canal / tunnel, cost analysis, and project projection using Retscreen software.
learning_outcomes: Learn the comprehensive design process of a small hydroelectric power plant.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Hydrological and site data collection
Hafta 2: Collection of hydrological and site data applications
Hafta 3: Collection of hydrological and site data project presentations
Hafta 8: Preliminary feasibility in power potential calculation
Hafta 9: Design of components (diversion weir and canal / pressure tunnel, head pond and penstock, turbine-generator set, generator, draft tube, tailrace canal / tunnel)
Hafta 10: Design of components (diversion weir and canal / pressure tunnel, head pond and penstock, turbine-generator set, generator, draft tube, tailrace canal / tunnel)
Hafta 11: Design of components (diversion weir and canal / pressure tunnel, head pond and penstock, turbine-generator set, generator, draft tube, tailrace canal / tunnel)
resources: Munson, Okiishi, Young, Fundamentals of Fluid Mechanics, Wiley.

courses.name: Yapı Dinamiğinin İncelenmesi
courses.purpose: Yapı sistemlerinin dinamiğini incelemek ve öğrencilerin yapıların dinamik davranışını analiz edebilmesini sağlamak.
courses.description: Yapı dinamiğinin temelleri, deprem ve rüzgar yükleri, tekli ve çoklu serbestlik derecesine sahip sönümlü ve sönümsüz yapıların bu yüklere verdiği tepkiler, yanıt spektrumu, Fourier analizi ve frekans bölgesi konuları işlenir.
learning_outcomes: Deneysel yapı dinamiği ilkelerini yapının tamamını hesaba katarak detaylı açıklayabilme
Uygun fiziksel modeli yansıtacak deney düzeneğini seçebilme
Analiz için gerekli girdileri tanımlayabilme ve ölçebilme
Yapı dinamiği parametrelerini çıkarabilmek için grafiksel ve nümerik eğri uydurma tekniklerini açıklayabilme ve kullanabilme
İlgili yazılımlardan uygun ileri modelleme parametrelerini çıkarabilme ve kullanabilme
Analiz sonuçlarını tasvir edebilme, örnek olarak, yapı dinamiği modeli
Bilinçli ve eleştirel olarak analiz sonuçlarının güvenilirliğini yargılayabilme
Sözlü ve yazılı olarak sonuçları raporlayabilme
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Faz 1 Teori: mekanik yapıların dinamik özelliklerini ölçme ve analizini yapma
Hafta 2: Faz 1 Teori: Analitik ve numerik yöntemler ile mekanik yapıların mod parametrelerini belirleme
Hafta 4: Faz 2 Uygulama: Zorlanmış hareketin analizi, birleşik yapıların analizi
Hafta 5: Faz 2 Uygulama: Hassasiyet (duyarlık) analizi, yapısal tadilatlar
Hafta 7: Faz 3 Bilgisayar uygulamaları: Deneysel datadan kirişin titreşim modlarının belirlenmesi
Hafta 8: Faz 3 Bilgisayar uygulamaları: Deneysel datadan kirişin titreşim modlarının belirlenmesi
Hafta 10: Faz 4 Mobilite fonksiyonlarının ölçümü, mod parametrelerinin frekanslarının tahmini
Hafta 11: Faz 4 mod sonuçlarının değerlendirilmesi ve sunumu

courses.name: Bridge Design
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to the design of simple and complex bridges, including beam, arch, and cable-stayed bridge types.
courses.description: The course covers different bridge types and their behavior, loads on bridges and load distribution, influence lines, design and analysis of composite bridges, arch bridges, cable-stayed bridges, and prestressed concrete bridges.
learning_outcomes: Classify bridges of different types.
Calculate loads, road factors, load cases, and load combinations.
Create and use influence lines for bridge analysis.
Explain the behavior of beam bridges, composite bridges, prestressed concrete bridges, arch bridges, and cable-stayed bridges.
Develop an ideal model for analysis and evaluate loads and moments.
Evaluate loads and moments in large displacements.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Phase 1: Introduction to bridge engineering preparations
Hafta 2: Phase 1: Bridge aesthetics and design process preparation, general design factors
Hafta 4: Phase 2: Determination of design loads: Materials. Review of design criteria. Loads on bridges.
Hafta 5: Phase 2: Determination of design loads: analysis tools
Hafta 8: Phase 3: Reinforced concrete bridge deck design
Hafta 10: Phase 4: Steel bridge deck design
Hafta 11: Phase 4: Design of bridge foundations
Hafta 12: Phase 4: Design of bridge piers

courses.name: Yapıların Sismik Performans Değerlendirmesi
courses.purpose: To understand seismic performance assessment methods for structures and how to apply them.
courses.description: Performance-based design process; updating methods and limitations; performance criteria; types of performance assessment; identification of earthquake risks; analysis of structural responses; establishing collapse fragility; performance calculation; decision making.
learning_outcomes: Define the methods and recommended procedures for possible seismic performance assessments according to the location, structural and non-structural, and occupancy characteristics of buildings.
Analyze structures in place, examine the results, and present them.
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: Phase 1: Updating methods and limitations
Hafta 5: Phase 2: Types of performance criteria
Hafta 7: Phase 3: Identification of earthquake risks

courses.name: Mevcut Yapıların Güçlendirilmesi
courses.purpose: Mevcut yapıların güçlendirilmesini anlamak ve uygulamak.
courses.description: Güçlendirme esasları, yapısal güçlendirme felsefesi, mevcut yapıların sismik güçlendirmesi ve güçlendirme ile enerji iyileştirmeleri ele alınmaktadır.
learning_outcomes: Güçlendirme esaslarını kavrama
Betonarme, çelik ve tarihi yapıların güçlendirmesi
Güçlendirme gerekliliğinin analizi
Proje tasarlama ve yönetme, analizini yapma ve sunma

Hafta 2: Faz 1: Güçlendirme esasları: yapıların kuvvetlendirilmesi/güçlendirilmesi ile ilgili yönetmelikler ve şartnameler

Hafta 8: Faz 3: Mevcut yapıların sismik güçlendirmesi: Çelik ve tarihi yapıların güçlendirilmesi

Hafta 11: Faz 4: Güçlendirme ve enerji güncellemeleri: Onarılmış binaların dış cephelerine yenilenebilir enerji teknolojilerinin uygulanması
Hafta 12: Bina enerji iyileştirmesi üzerine karar verme
Hafta 13: Enerji iyileştirmede bina tesislerinin rolü

courses.name: Oturma Sorunu
courses.purpose: Bu dersin amacı öğrencilere jeoloji, zemin mekaniği ve temel mühendisliği prensiplerini kullanarak zeminin yük altında aşırı oturmaya yatkın olduğu bir yerde temel tasarlamayı öğretmektir.
courses.description: Bu proje 5 aşamadan oluşmaktadır: Aşama 1 idealize edilmiş zemin profilinin oluşturulması, Aşama 2 sahadaki zemin için laboratuvar test sonuçlarının yorumlanması, Aşama 3 uygun temel ve zemin iyileştirme tekniğinin seçimi, Aşama 4 taşıma kapasitesi ve toplam oturma miktarının hesaplanması (veya eksenel kapasite ve oturmanın hesaplanması), Aşama 5 temel boyutlarının kesinleştirilmesi.
learning_outcomes: Yazılı ve sözlü olarak etkin iletişim kurabilecek
Analiz yapmak ve kararlara ulaşmak için ekiplerde etkin ve saygılı bir şekilde çalışabilecek
Veri toplayabilecek, uygun laboratuvar ve saha testlerini tayin edebilecek ve bu testlerin sonuçlarını yorumlayabilecek
Mühendislik hesaplamaları yapabilecek ve temel tipine karar verebilecek
Proje ile ilgili tüm verileri değerlendirerek temelin boyutlarını tasarlayabilecek
Taşıma kapasitesini artırmak ve yerleşimleri azaltmak için uygun bir zemin iyileştirme tekniği seçebilecek

courses.name: Deep Excavations and Retaining Structures
courses.purpose: The purpose of this course is to teach students how to design retaining structures in depth by applying principles of geology, soil mechanics, and foundation engineering.
courses.description: This project consists of five stages: Stage 1: Idealized soil profile; Stage 2: Interpretation of laboratory test results from the site soil; Stage 3: Selection of a suitable retaining structure for excavation; Stage 4: Calculation of lateral earth pressures and preliminary design; Stage 5: Completion of the retaining structure design.
learning_outcomes: Communicate effectively in written and oral form
Work effectively and respectfully in teams to analyze and reach decisions
Collect data, determine appropriate laboratory and field tests, and interpret the results of these tests
Perform engineering calculations and decide on the foundation type
Calculate lateral earth pressures
Design the retaining structure by evaluating all data collected for the project
Prepare a project file

courses.name: Innovative Materials in Civil Engineering Applications
courses.purpose: This is an elective design course.
courses.description: This course covers the following main topics in detail: introduction and basic concepts; sustainable and environmentally friendly materials; structural and functional performance; new technologies and case studies; research, testing, and future perspectives.
learning_outcomes: Understand and classify innovative materials.
Analyze the properties and applications of innovative materials.
Evaluate the sustainability and environmental benefits of new materials.
Apply new technologies in material development.
Perform basic laboratory tests on innovative materials.
Develop problem-solving skills for real-world applications.
Stay current with future trends and research directions.

courses.name: Data Science
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide general and task-specific knowledge on analyzing any kind of data to obtain useful information. Students will work on a problem in a hands-on manner using data mining techniques to extract such information. By the end of the course, students are expected to become beginner-level data scientists who understand different data types and their characteristics, can preprocess data for the main task, and can write code to extract useful information from different data sources.
courses.description: The course will cover classical machine learning, deep learning, natural language processing, and computer vision.
learning_outcomes: Ability to know, preprocess, and analyze data.
Ability to visualize data.
Ability to understand and analyze supervised/unsupervised learning methods such as classification, clustering, and regression.
Ability to use a programming language such as Python and its libraries to perform tasks on data such as classification, clustering, regression, and pattern mining.

Hafta 13: Machine learning and Naïve Bayes

Gain fundamental knowledge about advertising agencies and their organizational structure
Understand advertising processes

courses.name: Basic Marketing for Advertising
courses.purpose: This course aims to help students understand the marketing functions within firms in the advertising sector and to learn how to use marketing knowledge in developing and implementing successful marketing strategies.
courses.description: This is a basic-level marketing course that focuses on the fundamental terms, frameworks, and approaches of contemporary marketing theory, as well as their applications in practical business environments.

Understand self-marketing.
Develop the ability to understand the marketing function, the importance of marketing for consumers, and to develop and implement business strategies.

courses.name: Reklamcılığın Öncüleri
courses.purpose: Bu dersi alan öğrencilerin reklam endüstrisinin tarihsel gelişimine tanık olmaları, reklamcılık sektörünün lider ajanslarını tanımaları, reklamcılık sektörünün kurucu isimlerine dair bir anlayış geliştirmeleri ve Y kuşağı tüketicisi ile reklamcılığın geçirdiği evrimi kavramaları beklenmektedir.
courses.description: Bu derste öğrenciler reklamcılık sektörünün gelişimi ve yükselişini önemli tarihsel yapıtaşlarını inceleyerek öğreneceklerdir. İletişim teknolojilerinin reklamcılık sektörünün içeriğine, yaratıcılığına ve stratejilerine etkilerini örnekleri inceleyerek kavrayacaklardır. Reklam şirketleri/ajansları, kurucuları ve kampanyaları hakkında derinlemesine analiz yapacaklardır. Ayrıca yerel ve uluslararası ajanslar üzerinde projeler aracılığıyla çalışma gerçekleştireceklerdir. Ders kapsamında öğrenciler reklamcılık endüstrisinin lider ajanslarını, müşterilerini ve ödüllü kampanyalarını tanıyacaklardır.
learning_outcomes: Reklamcılığı tarihsel perspektifle bütüncül bir şekilde kavramak
Reklamcılık endüstrisinde yer alan reklam şirket/ajanslarını tanımak
Her ajansın marka değerine, şirket profiline ve müşterileri hakkında bilgi sahibi olmak
Reklamcılık ajansları ile temasa geçmek ve ilgili departmanlarla görüşmeler gerçekleştirmek
Ajansların müşteri ve trendlerle yakaladıkları değişimi gözlemek

courses.name: Communication Principles and Theories
courses.purpose: This course aims to help students understand the fundamental concepts of communication, build bridges between basic communication approaches and contemporary media phenomena, and analyze popular phenomena in social media.
courses.description: This course provides a comprehensive overview of the fundamental concepts and theoretical frameworks that form the basis of the field of communication, including political communication. Students explore various principles related to interpersonal, organizational, mass, and political communication and learn how these principles shape the ways individuals and groups interact and disseminate information. In addition, the course examines leading communication theories that have contributed to the development of the discipline and enables students to critically analyze and evaluate their effectiveness in different contexts. Through a combination of theoretical discussions, case studies, and practical applications, students develop a deep understanding of core principles and theories. For this purpose, the course is divided into three modules: strong media approaches, active-audience approaches, and post-structuralist media approaches. After each module, students are expected to develop an empirical project on a relevant media and/or communication phenomenon. Review activities focus on revisiting, further exploring, and practically applying the theories presented in class.
learning_outcomes: Develop an advanced understanding of important communication and media approaches.
Analyze some popular media and communication phenomena in terms of the relevant approaches.
Develop strategic and creative communication through skills such as writing, oral presentation, strategy development, creative and critical thinking.
Use the tools necessary for critical, analytical, and creative thinking skills and self-management processes enriched by academic research methods.
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: Basic Principles of Communication - Basic Communication Modules
Hafta 5: Frankfurt School and Culture Industry
Hafta 13: Media in the Control Society
resources: Mass communication theories: explaining origins, processes and effects. Margaret DeFleur & Melvin DeFleur
Dialectic of Enlightenment. Max Horkheimer & Theodor Adorno.
Understanding Media: Extensions of Man. Marshall McLuhan.
Manufacturing Consent: The Political Economy of the Mass Media. Edward Herman & Noam Chomsky.
Encoding and Decoding in the Television Discourse. Stuart Hall.
The Consumer Society: Myths and Structures. Jean Baudrillard.
The Second Media Age. Mark Poster.
What is the creative act?. Gilles Deleuze.
Griffin, E. (2012). A First Look at Communication Theory. McGraw-Hil

courses.name: Consumer Insight
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with a basic understanding of the concepts and tools used in customer analysis for consumer-oriented (B2C) businesses, to develop foundational qualitative market research knowledge for consumer insights, to understand how consumers think, and to understand the social and cultural determinants of consumer behavior.
courses.description: In this course, students learn how to integrate all marketing communication elements to understand, respond to, and/or change consumer behavior. Students will gain experience in developing consumer data-driven segmentation, targeting, and positioning strategies.
learning_outcomes: Understand how consumers make decisions.
Develop basic knowledge of how consumers shop in retail environments.
Gain the ability to uncover consumers' real motives, needs, and wants.
Understand the role of emotions and feelings in consumer behavior.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Orientation Week (Introduction and course plan)
Hafta 3: Qualitative and quantitative market research for consumer insights
Hafta 4: Target audience, segmentation and brand positioning
Hafta 8: Social and cultural determinants of consumer behavior
Hafta 10: Buying Process and Shopping Behavior

courses.name: Reklamda Yaratıcılık
courses.purpose: Bu dersi alan öğrencilerin yaratıcılığı öğrenmeleri, yaratıcılık konusunda deneyim kazanmaları ve reklamda yaratıcı olabilmeleri beklenmektedir.
courses.description: Bu ders, yaratıcılığın teorik ve pratik yönlerini ve yaratıcılık için düşünme sürecini ele alır. Yaratıcılığın doğuştan gelen bir yetenek olmak zorunda olmadığını, meraklı ve çalışkan bireyler tarafından geliştirilebileceğini açıklayarak yaratıcı insanların kapsamlı bir tanımını sunar. Ayrıca öğrencilerin yaratıcılığın bileşenlerini keşfetmesini, yaratıcı fikri ve yaratıcı süreci anlamasını ve yaratıcı çalışmaların değerlendirilmesini hedefler.

Yaratıcı reklamcının kim olduğunu açıklayabilme
Yaratıcı bir fikrin ne olduğunu anlama yeteneği
Yaratıcı süreç hakkında bilgi edinme becerisi
Yaratıcılığı, özellikle “yaratıcı reklam fikri”ni nasıl değerlendireceğini öğrenme becerisi
Öğrencilerin reklam yaratıcılığına ilişkin yaratıcı potansiyellerini anlamalarına yardımcı olan etkileşimli bir öğrenme ortamına katılma yeteneği

Hafta 10: Yaratıcı bir araç olarak hikaye anlatımını kullanmak
Hafta 13: Duygusal ve Akılcı faydaları kullanma

courses.name: Reklamcılık için Stratejik Araştırma
courses.purpose: Bu dersin amacı, öğrencilerin marka iletişiminin farklı aşamalarında kullanılan araştırma model ve yöntemlerini tanımasını sağlamak; reklam kampanyası geliştirme sürecinde araştırma temelli problem çözme yaklaşımı kazandırmak; araştırma bulgularından tüketici içgörüsü ve yaratıcı kampanya fikirleri üretme becerilerini geliştirmektir. Ayrıca öğrencilerin yaratıcı metodolojiler geliştirerek saha çalışması yürütebilmesi ve bulguları yorumlayabilmesi hedeflenir.
courses.description: Bu ders, reklam fikrinin geliştirilmesinden önceki aşamalar ile reklam sonrası etkinliğin ölçümünde kullanılan yöntemlere genel bir giriş sunar. Ders kapsamında reklam öncesi tüketici algılarını anlamaya yönelik araştırmalar, iletişim stratejisi oluşturmadan önce gerekli araştırma yöntemleri, konsept fikri geliştirme süreçleri ve pre/post reklam testleri, ürün ve ambalaj testleri ile yeni ürün geliştirme ve inovasyon testleri gibi araştırma yöntemleri ele alınır. Dersin odağı, reklam kampanyalarının temelinde pazarlama kural ve kavramlarının bulunduğunu kavratmak ve stratejik düşüncenin nasıl oluştuğunu göstermektir.
learning_outcomes: İletişim fikirlerinin oluşturulmasına yönelik araştırma bazlı anlayışı geliştirmek.
Reklam araştırmalarının temelini oluşturan araştırma yöntemlerinin nasıl işlediğini öğrenmek.
Reklam kampanyası öncesi strateji ve içgörü oluşturulmasının aşamalarını kavramak.

Tüketiciden verilerin hangi yöntemlerle ne şekilde elde edileceğini anlamak ve toplanan verilerle sentez ve yorum yapabilecek bilgileri proje odaklı içeriklerle hazırlamak ve sunma becerisi elde etmek.

courses.name: Research Design
courses.purpose: This course aims to help students understand the fundamentals of research design principles, collect quantitative and qualitative data, analyze data, and present research findings. Students are expected to carry out a complete research process from start to finish.

Ability to understand the stages of developing relevant research methods before fieldwork.

courses.name: Reklamcılıkta Sahneleme
courses.purpose: Bu dersin amacı, öğrencilerin reklamın temel kavramlarını anlamalarını, reklam ajanslarının organizasyon yapısı hakkında temel bilgi edinmelerini ve reklamcılığın pazarlama karması içindeki yerini ile işlevini kavramalarını sağlamaktır.

courses.name: Advertising Development
courses.purpose: This course aims to help students witness the historical development of the advertising industry, become familiar with leading agencies in the field, develop an understanding of the founding figures of advertising, and examine the evolution of advertising in relation to the consumer of the Y generation.
courses.description: In this course, students will learn about the development and rise of the advertising industry by examining key historical milestones. They will understand how communication technologies have affected the content, creativity, and strategies of the advertising sector through examples. Students will conduct in-depth analyses of advertising companies/agencies, their founders, and campaigns. Through projects, they will work on local and international agencies. The course also introduces leading agencies, their clients, and award-winning campaigns.
learning_outcomes: Understand advertising from a historical perspective in a holistic way
Recognize advertising companies/agencies in the advertising industry
Gain knowledge about each agency's brand value, company profile, and clients
Contact advertising agencies and conduct meetings with relevant departments
Observe the changes agencies achieve with clients and trends
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Orientation week (Introduction and course plan)
Hafta 3: The evolution of mass communication
Hafta 6: The development of advertising and Madison Avenue
Hafta 13: The millennium consumer and branding
resources: All required/recommended readings will be provided digitally via KHASLearn and/or KHAS Library.

courses.name: Digital Advertising
courses.purpose: This course aims to help students understand the fundamentals of digital advertising and the digital advertising ecosystem. It also seeks to develop strategic thinking, observation, data analysis, problem identification, insight generation, and storytelling skills, while enabling students to analyze digital platforms and products and apply theoretical knowledge through assignments and projects.
courses.description: This course provides an introductory overview of concepts in the digital advertising ecosystem and focuses on how effective advertising communication and marketing can be achieved through digital channels by analyzing consumers' emotional, psychological, and behavioral patterns. It also introduces digital advertising strategies and tools in depth and covers the basic principles of creating effective digital advertising campaigns.
learning_outcomes: Observe, analyze, and conduct planning, communication, and advertising effectively in digital advertising media
Create customer journey maps
Create personas
Develop effective digital marketing strategies
Transform digital strategies into measurable actions
Design landing pages

Hafta 4: Traditional Advertising vs. Digital Advertising
Hafta 7: Display, Search Engine and Social Media Advertising
Hafta 8: Video, E-Mail and Content Advertising
Hafta 13: Digital advertising trends and the future of digital advertising

The Power of Habit - Charles Duhigg
The Innovators - Walter Isaacson
The Fourth Industrial Revolution - Klaus Shwab

courses.name: Advertising Theories
courses.purpose: This course aims to help students understand and explain fundamental advertising theories and models, analyze the effects of advertising on consumer behavior and society, and develop critical thinking skills when evaluating advertising strategies and campaigns. Students are also expected to apply theoretical concepts to the creation and critique of effective advertising campaigns.
courses.description: This course covers fundamental advertising theories and models, the impact of advertising on consumer behavior and society, and the critical evaluation of advertising strategies and campaigns. It also introduces the application of theoretical concepts to the development and critique of effective advertising campaigns.
learning_outcomes: Explain and distinguish among various advertising theories and models.
Analyze the effectiveness of advertising strategies using theoretical frameworks.
Apply advertising theories to the development of advertising campaigns.
Conduct research on advertising issues and present findings effectively.
Critically evaluate the social and ethical implications of advertising.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Orientation Week (Introduction and course plan)
Hafta 2: What does Advertising Theory mean?
Hafta 6: How does culture work in advertising?
Hafta 10: Social Media and Advertising Theory

courses.name: Consumer Culture
courses.purpose: This course aims to help students understand the basic concepts of consumer culture and consumer research, gain knowledge about the consumer, market system, individual and mass consumption, and their consequences within the framework of consumer culture theory, and recognize the reflections of consumer culture in advertising theory and practice as well as their impact on society.
courses.description: This course covers various critiques of the market, consumption practices in the capitalist order, the encounter between everyday life and identity in the capitalist order, subculture groups and their relations with the system, and content related to market-consumer policies. By completing the course, the student develops a perspective on how the concepts of consumption and advertising are addressed in advertising and marketing literature through relevant conceptual references.
learning_outcomes: Understand the concept of consumption and consumer culture from different perspectives and develop critical viewpoints on these concepts.
Gain knowledge about how the concept of consumption is addressed in different disciplines and what the critiques are.
Use information about the market system and consumer culture to both critique this relationship and understand application areas in advertising and marketing.

Acquire the ability to understand innovative ideas in the advertising sector
Understand changing consumer behavior patterns
Be able to discuss increasing a brand's value through advertising practices

Understand and analyze current media phenomena
Develop strategic and creative communication literacy

Reklamcılığın süreçlerini anlamak

Ability to understand well-planned and fluent campaigns
Ability to analyze real advertising campaigns and prepare for a career in advertising

courses.name: Reklam Stratejisi
courses.purpose: This course aims to help students gain the knowledge and skills needed to plan and manage customer relationships by examining the concepts and issues of advertising in depth within a strategic planning framework. It also aims to develop strategic thinking, writing, and presentation skills.
courses.description: This course introduces the basic understanding of advertising strategy. It covers the relationships among functions in advertising agencies, the relationship between creative teams and brand strategists, current advertising literature, strategic brand moves, strategic thinking, strategic writing, and strategic communication.
learning_outcomes: Understand what strategic planning is.
Be able to plan marketing research and interpret the results.
Be able to write a creative brief based on consumer insight.

courses.name: Art Direction
courses.purpose: This course aims to enable students to apply advertising visual techniques and to gain the skills needed to support current professional practice in the field.

Understand the importance of using photography and illustration in printed advertisements.
Classify the concepts of corporate identity, design, and image.
Design a typeface based on specific typographic knowledge.
Develop a comprehensive corporate image.

courses.name: Text Writing
courses.purpose: The course aims to help students develop their creativity and creative writing skills. It also aims to provide experience in generating, improving, and applying ideas by examining current developments in the industry.
courses.description: The course meets the needs of students planning professional and administrative careers in advertising, copywriting, marketing, and business. It introduces the role of the copywriter and the practice of writing for different advertising channels.
learning_outcomes: Apply copywriting techniques for different advertising channels.
Apply copywriting techniques for different advertising channels.
Understand and apply the copywriting process in advertising agencies.

Hafta 2: Copywriting: Who is a copywriter? What is their daily routine? How do they work in agency hierarchy?
Hafta 3: How to find an idea?: Does everything start with an idea? Can creativity be learned? Who is creative? How does an idea develop? How is an idea killed?
Hafta 4: Brief writing/reading: What is a creative brief? How does a copywriter write their own brief? How does it help them?
Hafta 5: Slogan and Tagline: What is the difference between slogan and tagline? How are headlines/slogans written? How do they emerge / multiply / die?
Hafta 6: Writing for print: How is a good idea turned into words? How can one advertise without using a single word?
Hafta 7: Writing for outdoor: What is the difference between print and outdoor? What makes a good outdoor advertisement?
Hafta 8: Writing TV / cinema commercials: How does a copywriter combine text, image, and sound? How is an impressive commercial written?

Hafta 10: Writing digital advertising: What is the difference? How are viral ads written? How are social media ads written?
Hafta 11: Writing new media advertising: Can a copywriter create their own medium? How is advertising written for new media channels such as guerrilla, ambient, and event?

courses.name: Media Planning
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to the basic dynamics and concepts of media planning and buying, help them develop media literacy, and provide an understanding of how to approach major media issues encountered in media planning. It also seeks to familiarize students with media planning and buying processes and the evaluation of media plans.
courses.description: This course covers the fundamentals of media planning and buying in general, with a focus on media concepts and planning processes integrated with media research and analysis.
learning_outcomes: Know the main media concepts and terminology
Read and evaluate media research
Calculate media cost-effectiveness metrics
Prepare an integrated, comprehensive media plan

courses.name: Advertising Analysis
courses.purpose: This course aims to enable students to master the basic principles and practices of analyzing an advertisement in any medium through Jib Fowles' 15 basic emotional appeals perspective. It also aims to help students understand how to evaluate an advertisement in order to identify which emotional appeal underlies the approach to and logic of analysis.
courses.description: This course examines Jib Fowles' emotional appeals approach, which is based on human psychological needs. Maslow's Hierarchy of Needs pyramid is studied to determine what constitutes a need. Then, through discussion of the instructor's notes and by watching advertisements from various media and sectors worldwide, each of the 15 appeals is explained. Guest speakers who are experts and experienced in the field of marketing visit the class during the semester and share their knowledge.
learning_outcomes: Ability to apply Fowles' psychological advertising analysis method to advertisements in different media
Ability to understand the logical, cautious, and skeptical powers consumers develop when they see an advertisement
Ability to distinguish the creative approach from the psychological approach when designing an advertisement
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Orientation Week (Introduction to the course plan)
resources: All required / recommended readings will be provided as a digital / reserve reading package (available on KHASLearn and/or KHAS Library).

courses.name: Customer Relationship Management
courses.purpose: This course aims to help students learn fundamental theories, methodologies, and communication strategies related to customer relationship management. It also seeks to develop strategic thinking skills, improve marketing knowledge through strategic business models, and enable students to identify and apply strategic models to analyze business problems and formulate appropriate solutions.
courses.description: This course provides students with a general understanding of customer relationship management (CRM) as a process of managing customer interactions. It includes organizing, automating, and synchronizing customer information related to sales, marketing, and customer support.
learning_outcomes: Develop insight into CRM applications and technologies to achieve marketing, sales, and service goals throughout the customer life cycle
Gain the ability to follow current issues in customer relationship management
Conduct industry interviews using primary research
Write a report for evaluation and cite sources, and present key points briefly and clearly
Develop critical thinking and presentation skills

courses.name: Digital Consumer Experience
courses.purpose: Students taking this course are expected to observe the digital transformation in the economy, understand how digital transformation changes traditional advertising and marketing, learn how to communicate with consumers in digital and phygital channels, understand how design, technology, and business goals should be balanced for innovative digital products, grasp the fundamentals of consumer and user experience, and develop basic qualitative data collection and analysis skills as well as basic visual communication skills.

Understand consumer experience and how it can be measured

courses.name: Interactive Advertising
courses.purpose: This course aims to help students understand the dynamics of interactive advertising and its impact on consumers. It also aims to develop students' ability to analyze the strategic and communication dimensions of online and offline interactive advertising and to build an understanding of key principles such as social media management, user experience, and content generation for persuasive interactive marketing tools.
courses.description: This course focuses on why interactive advertising is essential in the 'Age of People' in communication and marketing. It introduces students to concepts, structures, and the most popular media used for interaction and engagement by analyzing best practices. The course covers digital campaigns, types of digital advertising, effective use of platforms such as YouTube, Facebook, InstaStories, and Twitter, mobile applications, social media management, gamification, virtual reality, and also offline interactive advertising such as shopper marketing, out-of-home, and guerrilla marketing.

Develop an understanding of how interactive advertising differs from conventional advertising both online and offline.
Learn the basic tools of interactive advertising, such as 3D technologies, and learn how to run an interactive campaign.
Examine and discuss the creative, design, and social aspects that shape interactive advertising.

courses.name: Visual Thinking in Advertising
courses.purpose: This course aims to help students understand the fundamentals of analyzing and critically reading various signs, symbols, and visuals in everyday life. Interpret examples of visual communication design from around the world and reach a level where they can apply these examples.
Evaluate the relationship between certain aspects of Gestalt Theory and visual communication.

courses.name: Advertising Management
